12-2.html.nextLabel: A matter to be considered when giving probation and penance.

12-3.html.title: A Matter to be Observed when Granting Probation and Penance.
12-3.html.body:
In certain instances, owing to a shortage of bhikkhus, it may be necessary to include bhikkhus who have previously undertaken probation or penance in the proceedings for granting the same to others. In such circumstances, one must first relinquish the probation or penance; one should not participate in granting probation or penance to another bhikkhu while still observing it. If a bhikkhu who is observing probation or penance is counted toward the quorum of the Community, the probation or penance being granted is not validly effected. It is an invalid act. This applies similarly to the performance of the act of rehabilitation.

pārivāsika catuttho ce bhikkhave! parivāsaṃ dadeyya. Mūlāya paṭikasseyya. Mānattaṃ dadeyya. Vīso abbheyya. Akammaṃ na ca karaṇīyaṃ. Mūlāya paṭikassanāraha catuttho ce bhikkhave! parivāsaṃ dadeyya. Mūlāya paṭikasseyya, mānattaṃ dadeyya, viso abbheyya, akammaṃ na ca karaṇīyaṃ. Mānattāraha catuttho ce bhikkhave! parivāsaṃ dadeyya, mūlāya paṭikasseyya, mānattaṃ dadeyya, vīso abbheyya, akammaṃ na ca karaṇīyaṃ. Mānattacārikacatuttho ce bhikkhave! parivāsaṃ dadeyya, mūlāya paṭikasseyya, mānattaṃ dadeyya. Vīso abbheyya, akammaṃ na ca karaṇīyaṃ, abbhānāraha catuttho ce bhikkhave! parivāsaṃ dadeyya, mūlāya paṭikasseyya, mānattaṃ dadeyya, vīso abbheyya. Akammaṃ na ca karaṇīyaṃ.

This is a teaching found in the Campeyya Khandhaka of the Mahāvagga Pāḷi.
12-3.html.prevLabel: The Method of Practice for Bhikkhus Fulfilling Duties.

12.html.title: Absolute Probation (suddhantaparivāsaya)
12.html.body:
A bhikkhu who has committed many offenses but knows neither the number of offenses nor the number of days they were concealed must fulfill Absolute Probation. Considering the duration he lived without announcing his offenses, he must serve probation for that period, then undertake the six-day penance (Mānatta), and finally undergo the Act of Rehabilitation (Abbhāna).

If he has never made an announcement of offenses since his higher ordination, and offenses have occurred, he must fulfill the duty for the entire duration since his higher ordination.

Formula for Requesting Absolute Probation.

Ahaṃ bhante! sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāmi. Rattipariyantaṃ na jānāmi. Āpattipariyantaṃ nassarāmi. Ratti pariyantaṃ nassarāmi. Āpattipariyante vematiko. Rattipariyante vematiko. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ yācāmi.

Recite three times.

The Formal Act Statement for Granting Probation.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajji. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāti, ratti pariyantaṃ na jānāti. Āpatti pariyantaṃ nassarati. Rattipariyantaṃ nassarati, āpattipariyante vematiko, rattipariyante vematiko. So saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ dadey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajji. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāti. Rattipariyantaṃ na jānāti. Āpattipariyantaṃ nassarati. Rattipariyantaṃ nassarati. Āpattipariyante vematiko. Rattipariyante vematiko. So saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ yācati. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhanta parivāsaṃ deti. Yassāyasmato khamati (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsassa dānaṃ. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati. So bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe- so bhāsey‍ya.

Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe- so bhāsey‍ya.

Dinno saṅghena (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāso. Khamati saṅghassa tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

Formula for Undertaking Probation.

Parivāsaṃ samādiyāmi. Vattaṃ samādiyāmi.

Undertake by reciting three times.

Formula for Announcement.

Ahaṃ bhante! sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāmi. Rattipariyantaṃ na jānāmi. Āpattipariyantaṃ nassarāmi. Rattipariyantaṃ nassarāmi. Āpatti pariyante vematiko. Rattipariyante vematiko. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ yāciṃ. Tassa me saṅgho tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ adāsi. So’haṃ parivasāmi vedayāmahaṃ bhante vedayatīti maṃ saṅgho dhāretu.

Announce this three times. When announcing to one, two, or three bhikkhus, use the appropriate grammatical variations. When suspending (Nikkhepa), say “Parivāsaṃ nikkhipāmi. Vattaṃ nikkhipāmi” three times. The suspension is effective even with one of these phrases. The Commentary states: "Even with one word, the probation is suspended here; but with two, it is well-suspended" (Ekapadenā’pi vet‍tha nikkhitto hoti parivāso. Dvīhi pana sunikkhitto yeva). Having taken probation in this manner, go to a secluded place, reflect on your past, and fulfill the duty for a sufficient number of days to remove all doubt before taking the penance (Mānatta). Since one with many offenses may also have unconcealed offenses, when taking the penance, combine the concealed offenses for which probation was served with any unconcealed offenses, and take the penance for all of them. If the probation was in a state of suspension, you must first re-undertake and announce the probation within the boundary before requesting the penance from the Community.

Formula for Requesting Penance.

Ahaṃ bhante! sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāmi. Rattipariyantaṃ na jānāmi. Āpattipariyantaṃ nassarāmi. Rattipariyantaṃ nassarāmi. Āpattipariyante vematiko. Rattipariyante vematiko. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ yāciṃ. Tassa me saṅgho tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ adāsi. So’haṃ bhante! parivuttha parivāso. Ahaṃ bhante! sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ. Appaṭicchannāyo. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ yācāmi.

Recite three times.

The Formal Act Statement for Granting Penance.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajji. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāti. Rattipariyantaṃ na jānāti. Āpattipariyantaṃ nassarati. Rattipariyantaṃ nassarati. Āpattipariyante vematiko. Rattipariyante vematiko. So saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhanta parivāsaṃ yāci. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ adāsi. So parivut‍thaparivāso. Ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ appaṭicchannāyo. So saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ dadey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajji. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāti. Rattipariyantaṃ na jānāti. Āpattipariyantaṃ nassarati. Rattipariyantaṃ nassarati. Āpattipariyante vematiko. Rattipariyante vematiko. So saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ yāci. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ adāsi. So parivut‍thaparivāso. Ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ appaṭicchannāyo. So saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ yācati. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ deti. Yassāyasmato khamati, (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattassa dānaṃ. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati so bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe- so bhāsey‍ya.

Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe- so bhāsey‍ya.

Dinnaṃ saṅghena (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ. Khamati saṅghassa tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

After receiving the penance, while still within the boundary, arrange the upper robe over one shoulder, sit in the squatting posture, pay homage to the Community, and undertake the penance by reciting “Mānattaṃ samādiyāmi. Vattaṃ samādiyāmi” three times, then announce it to the Community.

Formula for Announcing the Penance.

Ahaṃ bhante! sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāmi. Rattipariyantaṃ na jānāmi. Āpattipariyantaṃ nassarāmi. Rattipariyantaṃ nassarāmi. Āpattipariyante vematiko. Ratti pariyante vematiko. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ yāciṃ. Tassa me saṅgho tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ adāsi. So’haṃ bhante! parivuttha parivāso. Ahaṃ bhante sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ appaṭicchannāyo. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ yāciṃ. Tassa me saṅgho tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ adāsi. So’haṃ mānattaṃ carāmi. Vedayāma’haṃ bhante vedayatīti maṃ saṅgho dhāretu.

Announce this three times. Thereafter, paying homage to the Community, say “Mānattaṃ nikkhipāmi. Vattaṃ nikkhipāmi” three times to suspend the penance. Then, go and reside in a monastery where there are not less than four bhikkhus, fulfilling the penance for six days before undergoing the Act of Rehabilitation (Abbhāna). To undergo rehabilitation, assemble a group of not less than twenty bhikkhus within the boundary. If the penance was in a state of suspension, re-undertake it within the boundary, announce it to the Community, and then request rehabilitation.

Formula for Requesting Rehabilitation for Absolute Probation.

Ahaṃ bhante! sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāmi. Rattipariyantaṃ na jānāmi. Āpattipariyantaṃ nassarāmi. Rattipariyantaṃ nassarāmi. Āpattipariyante vematiko. Rattipariyante vematiko. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ yāciṃ. Tassa me saṅgho tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ adāsi. So’haṃ bhante parivutthaparivāso. Ahaṃ bhante! sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ appaṭicchannāyo. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ yāciṃ. Tassa me saṅgho tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ adāsi. So’haṃ bhante! ciṇṇamānatto saṅghaṃ abbhānaṃ yācāmi.

Arrange the upper robe over one shoulder, sit in the squatting posture, pay homage to the Community, and request rehabilitation by reciting this formula three times.

The Formal Act Statement for Rehabilitation for Absolute Probation.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajji. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāti. Rattipariyantaṃ na jānāti. Āpattipariyantaṃ nassarati. Rattipariyantaṃ nassarati. Āpattipariyante vematiko. Rattipariyante vematiko. So saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ yāci. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ adāsi. So parivut‍thaparivāso. Ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ appaṭicchannāyo. So saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ yāci. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ adāsi. So ciṇṇamānatto saṅghaṃ abbhānaṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ. Saṅgho tissaṃ bhikkhuṃ abbhey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajji. Āpattipariyantaṃ na jānāti. Rattipariyantaṃ na jānāti. Āpattipariyantaṃ nassarati. Rattipariyantaṃ nassarati. Āpattipariyante vematiko. Rattipariyante vematiko. So saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ yāci. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ āpattīnaṃ suddhantaparivāsaṃ adāsi. So parivut‍thaparivāso, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu sambahulā saṅghādisesā āpattiyo āpajjiṃ appaṭicchannāyo. So saṅghaṃ tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ yāci. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno tāsaṃ sambahulānaṃ āpattīnaṃ paṭicchannānañca appaṭicchannānañca chārattaṃ mānattaṃ adāsi. So ciṇṇamānatto saṅghaṃ abbhānaṃ yācati. Saṅgho (tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ abbheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (tissassa) bhikkhuno abbhānaṃ. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati. So bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe- so bhāsey‍ya.

Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe- so bhāsey‍ya.

Abbhito saṅghena (tisso) bhikkhu. Khamati saṅghassa tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

There are numerous methods in the Vinaya for obtaining purification from grave offenses (Garukāpatti). Three methods are shown here, which are currently in use among bhikkhus. Through these three methods, purification from all grave offenses can be achieved. The Formal Act Statements provided here use the name ‘Tissa’. In performing a Vinaya Act for a bhikkhu, his actual name should be substituted wherever ‘Tissa’ appears in the Formal Act Statement. If there is a difficulty in doing so, the act-proclaimer may temporarily designate the name of the candidate as ‘Tissa’ and proceed with the statement as written. To designate the name, the act-proclaimer should state:

"Venerable Sirs, for the ease of proclaiming the Formal Act Statement, I designate the name ‘Tissa’ for the bhikkhu [Name], setting aside his former name. May the Community be aware of this. Let the candidate also be aware."

In this manner, the name must be designated. Once the name is designated, the Formal Act Statement must be proclaimed exactly as it appears in the book.

Sub-topics

  • Facts to be known regarding the fulfillment of Duties (Vat) and Penance (Mānat).
  • The code of conduct for Bhikkhus fulfilling Duties (Vat).
  • A point to be considered when granting Vat-mānat.
12.html.nextLabel: Facts regarding the fulfillment of Duties

13.html.title: Probation for Sectarians
13.html.body:
If a naked sectarian comes seeking the Going-forth and Higher Ordination in the Buddha’s Dispensation, give him the Going-forth and let him observe a four-month probation. He should be granted Higher Ordination only after he has completed and fulfilled the four-month probation. The one who was formerly a member of another sect, having approached the Community, having arranged his upper robe over one shoulder, having paid homage to the feet of the bhikkhus, and having sat in the squatting position, should request probation thus:

Ahaṃ bhante! (tisso) aññatitthiyapubbo imasmiṃ dhammavinaye ākaṅkhāmi upasampadaṃ. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ cattāro māse parivāsaṃ yācāmi. Dutiyampi ahaṃ bhante -pe- tatiyampi ahaṃ bhante -pe- parivāsaṃ yācāmi.

The Formal Act for Granting Probation.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Ayaṃ (tisso) aññatit‍thiyapubbo imasmiṃ dhammavinaye ākaṅkhati upasampadaṃ. So saṅghaṃ cattāro māse parivāsaṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ. Saṅgho (tissassa) aññatit‍thiyapubbassa cattāro māse parivāsaṃ dadey‍ya, esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) aññatit‍thiya pubbo imasmiṃ dhammavinaye ākaṅkhati upasampadaṃ, so saṅghaṃ cattāro māse parivāsaṃ yācati. Saṅgho (tissassa) aññatit‍thiya pubbassa cattāro māse parivāsaṃ deti. Yassāyasmato khamati (tissassa) aññatit‍thiya pubbassa cattāro māse parivāsassa dānaṃ, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati so bhāsey‍ya. Dinno saṅghena (tissassa) aññatit‍thiya pubbassa cattāro māse parivāso. Khamati saṅghassa tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

After he has satisfied the bhikkhus during the four-month probation, the one who was formerly a member of another sect should be given Higher Ordination. Further details should be known from the Mahakhandhaka.
13.html.prevLabel: Matters to be observed when giving the Observance and Mānatta.
13.html.nextLabel: The Formal Act for Designating a Site for a Hut

14.html.title: The Act of Assigning a Site for a Hut
14.html.body:
The bhikkhu building the hut should clear the site for the hut, approach the Community, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay homage at the feet of the senior bhikkhus, sit on his heels, raise his joined palms, and request the Community three times as follows:

“Ahaṃ bhante! saññācikāya kuṭiṃ kattukāmo assāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ kuṭivatthuolokanaṃ yācāmi.”

When the bhikkhu building the hut has thus requested, if the Community wishes to go and inspect the site, it is well. If not, the Community should authorize competent bhikkhus who are capable of discerning whether there are obstructions or a lack of open space, and send them to inspect the site.

The Formal Act of Authorization.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu saññācikāya kuṭiṃ kattukāmo assāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ. So saṅghaṃ kuṭivat‍thu olokanaṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (buddharakkhitañca dhammarakkhitañca) bhikkhu sammanney‍ya (tissassa) bhikkhuno kuṭivat‍thuṃ oloketaṃ: esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu saññācikāya kuṭiṃ kattukāmo assāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ. So saṅghaṃ kuṭivat‍thu olokanaṃ yācati. Saṅgho (buddharakkhitañca dhammarakkhitañca) bhikkhu sammannati (tissassa) bhikkhuno kuṭivat‍thuṃ oloketuṃ. Yassāyasmato khamati (buddharakkhitassa ca dhammarakkhitassa ca) bhikkhūnaṃ sammuti (tissassa) bhikkhuno kuṭivat‍thuṃ oloketuṃ, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Sammatā saṅghena (buddharakkhito ca dhammarakkhito ca) bhikkhu (tissassa) bhikkhuno kuṭivat‍thu oloketuṃ, khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

The authorized bhikkhus should go to the site where the hut is to be built and investigate whether it is a place with obstructions, and whether there is enough open space for a cart yoked with two oxen to pass around the hut. If it is a place with obstructions or lacks the required open space, they should inform the bhikkhu building the hut that he should not build there. If the site is suitable and appropriate for building a hut, they should inform the Community. Thereafter, the bhikkhu building the hut should approach the Community, pay homage at the feet of the senior bhikkhus, sit on his heels, raise his joined palms, and request the assignment of the site for the hut.

The Request for the Assignment of the Site for a Hut.

“Ahaṃ bhante! saññacikāya kuṭiṃ kattukāmo assāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ kuṭivatthudesanaṃ yācāmi. Dutiyampi -pe- tatiyampi -pe- yācāmi.”

The Formal Act for Assigning the Site for a Hut.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu saññācikāya kuṭiṃ kattukāmo assāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ, so saṅghaṃ kuṭivat‍thudesanaṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno kuṭivat‍thu desey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu saññācikāya kuṭiṃ kattukāmo assāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ. So saṅghaṃ kuṭivat‍thu desanaṃ yācati. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno kuṭivat‍tuṃ deseti. Yassāyasmato khamati (tissassa) bhikkhuno kuṭivat‍thu desanā, so tuṇhassa, yassa nakkhamati so bhāsey‍ya.

Desitaṃ saṅghena (tissassa) bhikkhuno kuṭivat‍thu. Khamati saṅghassa. Tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

The purpose of having the site assigned is to obtain the formal approval of the Community to build a hut or a dwelling. A bhikkhu who builds a hut for his own use with materials he himself has begged for, without having the site assigned, commits an offense of Sanghadisesa.

The Assignment of a Site for a Large Dwelling.

A bhikkhu who is building or having a large dwelling built with a benefactor must also have the site assigned by the Community before proceeding. If he builds the dwelling without doing so, he commits an offense of Sanghadisesa.

The Request for Inspection of the Site.

Ahaṃ bhante! mahallakaṃ vihāraṃ kattukāmo sassāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ. So’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ vihāravatthu olokanaṃ yācāmi. Dutiyampi -pe- tatiyampi -pe-

The Formal Act of Authorization for Inspecting the Site for a Dwelling.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu mahallakaṃ vihāraṃ kattukāmo sassāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ. So saṅghaṃ vihāravat‍thu olokanaṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ. Saṅgho (buddharakkhitañca dhammarakkhitañca) bhikkhu sammanney‍ya (tissassa) bhikkhuno vihāravat‍thuṃ oloketuṃ. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu mahallakaṃ vihāraṃ kattukāmo sassāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ. So saṅghaṃ vihāravat‍thu vilokanaṃ yācati. Saṅgho (buddharakkhitañca dhammarakkhitañca) bhikkhu sammannati (tissassa) bhikkhuno vihāravat‍tuṃ oloketuṃ yassāyasmato khamati (buddharakkhitassa ca dhammarakkhitassa ca) bhikkhūnaṃ sammuti (tissassa) bhikkhuno vihāravat‍thuṃ oloketuṃ so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati. So bhāsey‍ya.

Sammatā saṅghena (buddharakkhito ca dhammarakkhito ca) bhikkhu (tissassa) bhikkhuno vihāravat‍thuṃ oloketuṃ. Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

The Request for the Assignment of the Site for a Dwelling.

“Ahaṃ bhante! mahallakaṃ vihāraṃ kattukāmo sassāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ, so’haṃ bhante! saṅghaṃ vihāravatthu desanaṃ yācāmi. Dutiyampi -pe- tatiyampi -pe-.”

The Formal Act for Assigning the Site for a Dwelling.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu mahallakaṃ vihāraṃ kattukāmo sassāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ. So saṅghaṃ vihāravat‍thu desanaṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno vihāravat‍thuṃ desey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu mahallakaṃ vihāraṃ kattukāmo sassāmikaṃ attuddesaṃ. So saṅghaṃ vihāravat‍thu desanaṃ yācati. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno vihāravat‍thuṃ deseti. Yassāyasmato khamati (tissassa) bhikkhuno vihāravat‍thu desanā, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati so bhāsey‍ya.

Desitaṃ saṅghena (tissassa) bhikkhuno vihāravat‍thu. Khamati saṅghassa tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

15.html.title: The Formal Act of Admonition
15.html.body:
If a certain bhikkhu attempts to cause a schism in the Community, or persists in taking up a matter that leads to a schism, the bhikkhus who meet that bhikkhu attempting the schism should advise him: "Venerable, do not attempt to cause a schism in the harmonious Community." You should advise him thus a second and a third time. Bhikkhus who hear reports of such a bhikkhu should also seek out the bhikkhu attempting the schism and advise him. If he hears the advice but does not relinquish his position, he commits an offence of wrongdoing (dukkaṭa). Bhikkhus who hear of the bhikkhu attempting the schism but do not advise him also commit an offence of wrongdoing (dukkaṭa). If the bhikkhu attempting the schism does not relinquish his position even after being advised, the other bhikkhus should bring him, even by force, into the midst of the Community and perform the Formal Act of Admonition (samanubhāsana kamma).

The Transaction Statement.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu samaggassa saṅghassa bhedāya parakkamati. So taṃ vat‍thuṃ na paṭinissajjati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ samanubhāsey‍ya tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu samaggassa saṅghassa bhedāya parakkamati. So taṃ vat‍thuṃ na paṭinissajjati. Saṅgho (tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ samanubhāsati tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. Yassāyasmato khamati (tissassa) bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe- so bhāsey‍ya.

Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu samaggassa saṅghassa bhedāya parakkamati. So taṃ vat‍thuṃ na paṭinissajjati. Saṅgho (tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ samanubhāsati tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. Yassāyasmato khamati (tissassa) bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati. So bhāsey‍ya

Samanubhaṭṭho saṅghena (tisso) bhikkhu tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

In performing this Formal Act of Admonition, if the bhikkhu attempting the schism does not relinquish his view, an offence of wrongdoing (dukkaṭa) is incurred at the conclusion of the Motion (ñatti). At the conclusion of the first proclamation (anusāvana), a grave offence (thullaccaya) is incurred. At the conclusion of the second proclamation, a grave offence (thullaccaya) is also incurred. At the conclusion of the transaction statement, an offence entailing initial and subsequent meetings of the Community (saṅghādisesa) is incurred.

The Formal Act of Admonition for Bhikkhus who side with Schismatics.

Bhikkhus who side with a bhikkhu attempting a schism should also be advised by other bhikkhus not to do so. You should advise them a second and a third time; if they do not accept the advice, they commit an offence of wrongdoing (dukkaṭa). Bhikkhus who know of this but do not advise them also commit an offence of wrongdoing (dukkaṭa). If they do not accept the advice, you should bring those bhikkhus into the midst of the Community, even by force, and advise them. If they still do not accept, the Formal Act of Admonition should be performed.

The Transaction Statement.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. (buddharakkhito ca dhammarakkhito ca) bhikkhu (tissassa) bhikkhuno saṅghabhedāya parakkamantassa anuvattakā vaggavādakā te taṃ vat‍thuṃ na paṭinissajjanti. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (buddharakkhitañca dhammarakkhitañca) bhikkhu samanubhāsey‍ya tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, (buddharakkhito ca dhammarakkhito ca) bhikkhu (tissassa) bhikkhuno saṅghabhedāya parakkamantassa anuvattakā vaggavādakā. Te taṃ vat‍thuṃ na paṭinissajjanti. Saṅgho (buddharakkhitañca dhammarakkhitañca) bhikkhu samanubhāsati tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. Yassāyasmato khamati (buddharakkhitassa ca dhammarakkhitassa ca) bhikkhūnaṃ samanubhāsanā tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe- so bhāsey‍ya. Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe- so bhāsey‍ya.

Samanubhaṭṭho saṅghena (buddharakkhito ca dhammarakkhito ca) bhikkhu tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

The Formal Act of Admonition for Difficult-to-admonish Bhikkhus.

When a bhikkhu is being advised by other bhikkhus according to the training rules laid down by the Buddha, such as: "Venerable, this should be done, this should not be done, you have fallen into such-and-such an offence," if that bhikkhu does not allow himself to be spoken to, saying: "Venerables, do not say anything to me, whether good or bad; you should mind your own business. I will not say anything to you, whether good or bad; do not say anything to me," then the bhikkhus should advise that bhikkhu not to act in that manner. You should advise him thus a second and a third time. If he does not accept the advice, he commits an offence of wrongdoing (dukkaṭa).

If the bhikkhus know of this but do not advise him, they also commit an offence of wrongdoing (dukkaṭa). If that bhikkhu does not accept the advice, he should be brought into the midst of the Community and advised. You should advise him a second and a third time. If he does not accept, the Formal Act of Admonition should be performed.

The Transaction Statement is as follows:

Suṇātu me bhante! ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṃ vuccamāno attānaṃ avacanīyaṃ karoti. So taṃ vat‍thuṃ na paṭinissajjati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ samanubhāsey‍ya tassa paṭinissaggāya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu bhikkhūhi sahadhammikaṃ vuccamāno attānaṃ avacanīyaṃ karoti. So taṃ vat‍thuṃ na paṭinissajjati. Saṅgho (tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ samanubhāsati tassa paṭinissaggāya. Yassāyasmato khamati (tissassa) bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. So tuṇhassa - yassa nakkhamati. So bhāsey‍ya. Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. -pe- tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi -pe-

Samanubhaṭṭho saṅghena (tisso) bhikkhu tassa vat‍thussa paṭinissaggāya. Khamati saṅghaşsa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

At the conclusion of this transaction statement, the difficult-to-admonish bhikkhu incurs an offence entailing initial and subsequent meetings of the Community (saṅghādisesa).
15.html.prevLabel: The Formal Act for Designating a Site for a Hut

16.html.title: The Act of Banishment
16.html.body:
The "Act of Banishment" (Pabbājanīya Kamma) refers to the removal of a bhikkhu from the monastery and village where he resides. It is to be carried out against bhikkhus who are corrupters of families. This formal act was first performed against the bhikkhus Assaji and Punabbasu who resided at Kīṭāgiri. Assaji and Punabbasu were two members of the group of six (chabbaggiya) bhikkhus. When performing this act, bring the bhikkhu who corrupts families into the midst of the Community, accuse him, remind him of the offense, and make him acknowledge the transgression before proceeding.

The Formal Motion for the Act of Banishment

suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ime (assaji punabbasukā) bhikkhu kuladūsakā pāpasamācārā. Imesaṃ pāpakā samācārā dissanti ceva suyyanti ca. Kulāni ca imehi duṭṭhāni dissanti ceva suyyanti ca. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ. Saṅgho (assaji punabbasukānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ (kīṭāgirismā) pabbājanīya kammaṃ kareyya. Na (assaji punabbasukehi) (kīṭāgirismiṃ) vatthabbanti. Esā ñatti.

suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ime (assaji punabbasukā) bhikkhu kuladūsakā pāpasamācārā. Imesaṃ pāpakā samācārā dissanti ceva suyyanti ca. Saṅgho (assaji punabbasukānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ (kīṭāgirismā) pabbājanīya kammaṃ karoti na (assaji punabbasukehi) bhikkhūhi. (kīṭāgirismiṃ) vatthabbanti. Yassāyasmato khamati (assaji punabbasukānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ (kīṭāgirismā) pabbājanīyakammassakaraṇaṃ, na (assaji punabbasukehi) bhikkhūhi (kīṭāgirismiṃ) vatthabbanti. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. Dutiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi, -pe- tatiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi. -pe-

Kataṃ saṅghena (Assaji-Punabbasukānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ (Kīṭāgirismā) pabbājanīya kammaṃ, na (Assaji-Punabbasukehi) bhikkhu (Kīṭāgirismiṃ) vatthabbanti. Khamati saṅghassa tasmā tuṇhī evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

There are eighteen duties to be observed by a bhikkhu against whom the Act of Banishment has been performed. When he has obediently fulfilled these eighteen duties and conducted himself properly, and requests the Community to revoke the Act of Banishment, the Community shall revoke it. Assaji and Punabbasu, however, did not conduct themselves properly under the Act of Banishment and began to revile the bhikkhus who had performed the act.

If a bhikkhu, having had the Act of Banishment performed against him for corrupting families, does not leave the monastery and reviles the bhikkhus who performed the act, saying, "These bhikkhus follow a wrong course through favoritism, etc. For such an offense they perform the Act of Banishment against some, but not against others," then other bhikkhus who see or hear this must admonish that bhikkhu up to three times to leave the monastery and to cease reviling the Community. If he does not desist, he must be brought before the Community and admonished. If he still does not desist before the Community, the Act of Formal Admonition (Samanubhāsana) must be performed against him.

The Formal Motion for the Act of Admonition

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ayaṃ (Tisso) bhikkhu saṅghena pabbājanīya kammakato, bhikkhū chandāgāmitā dosāgāmitā mohāgāmitā bhayāgāmitā pāpeti. So taṃ vatthuṃ na paṭinissajjati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ. Saṅgho (Tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ samanubhāseyya tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ayaṃ (Tisso) bhikkhū saṅghena pabbājanīya kammakato. Bhikkhū chandāgāmitā dosāgāmitā mohāgāmitā bhayāgāmitā pāpeti. So vatthuṃ na paṭinissajjati. Saṅgho (Tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ samanubhāsati tassa paṭinissaggāya. Yassāyasmato khamati (Tissassa) bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. Dutiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi -pe- Tatiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi -pe-.

Samanubhaṭṭho saṅghena (Tisso) bhikkhu tassa vatthussa paṭinissaggāya. Khamati saṅghassa tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

Upon the conclusion of this formal motion, the bhikkhus who revile those who performed the Act of Banishment incur an offense of Saṅghādisesa.

Revocation of the Act of Banishment

If a bhikkhu against whom the Act of Banishment was performed resided in a certain monastery and corrupted families in a certain village, he must not wander for alms in that village while residing in that monastery. While residing in that monastery, he must not wander for alms in any other village. Nor should he wander for alms in that village while residing in another monastery. The bhikkhu under the Act of Banishment must fulfill the prescribed eighteen duties. There is no fixed time for fulfilling these duties; even five days may suffice. Having fulfilled the duties, that bhikkhu should approach the Community, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay homage at the feet of the senior bhikkhus, sit in the squatting position, and with joined palms, make the request for the revocation of the Act of Banishment.

The Formula for Requesting Revocation

Ahaṃ bhante! saṅghena pabbājanīya kammakato. Sammā vattāmi. Lomaṃ pātemi. Netthāraṃ vattāmi. Pabbājanīya kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācāmi. Dutiyampi -pe- yācāmi. Tatiyampi -pe- yācāmi.

The Formal Motion for Revoking the Act

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ayaṃ (Tisso) bhikkhu saṅghena pabbājanīya kammakato. Sammāvattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Netthāraṃ vattati. Pabbājanīya kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (Tissassa) bhikkhuno pabbājanīya kammaṃ paṭippassambheyya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ayaṃ (Tisso) bhikkhu pabbājanīya kammakato. Sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Netthāraṃ vattati. Pabbājanīya kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (Tissassa) bhikkhuno pabbājanīyakammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (Tissassa) bhikkhuno pabbājanīya kammassa paṭippassaddhi. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya. Dutiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi. -pe- Tatiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi.

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho. Ayaṃ (Tisso) bhikkhu saṅghena pabbājanīya kammakato. Sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Netthāraṃ vattati. Pabbājanīya kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (Tissassa) bhikkhuno pabbājanīya kammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati. (Tissassa) bhikkhuno pabbājanīya kammassa paṭippassaddhi, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya.

Paṭippassaddhaṃ saṅghena (Tissassa) bhikkhuno pabbājanīyakammaṃ. Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

After the revocation of the Act of Banishment, that bhikkhu is permitted to reside in that monastery. However, that bhikkhu must not accept the four requisites from the families he corrupted. Even if that bhikkhu later becomes an Arahant, he must not accept requisites from those families even after his attainment. For as long as he lives, requisites from those families are unallowable for him. If the people of those families ask, "Venerable sir, why do you not accept the requisites we offer?" and he replies, "Because I previously corrupted your families," and those people say, "We do not give because of that, but now we give out of respect for your virtue," then there is no offense in accepting requisites from them thereafter.
16.html.nextLabel: The Act of Charging with a Different Offense

17.html.title: The Formal Act of Charging with Evasive Speech
17.html.body:
The Formal Act of Charging with Evasive Speech is a procedure prescribed for those bhikkhus who, having committed improper actions and fallen into offences, attempt to conceal their fault by speaking of something else when questioned in the midst of the Community.

The formal text of the act is as follows:

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu saṅghamajjhe āpattiyā anuyuñjiyamāno aññenaññaṃ paṭicarati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno aññavādakaṃ ropey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu saṅghamajjhe āpattiyā anuyuñjiyamāno aññenaññaṃ paṭicarati. Saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno aññavādakaṃ ropeti. Yassāyasmato khamati (channassa) bhikkhuno aññavādakassa ropanā. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Ropitaṃ saṅghena (channassa) bhikkhuno aññavādakaṃ. Khamati saṅghassa. Tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

Prior to the performance of this formal act, speaking evasively when questioned in the midst of the Community entails an offence of wrongdoing (dukkaṭa). After the charge of evasive speech has been formally laid, speaking evasively entails an offence to be confessed (pācittiya).
17.html.nextLabel: The Formal Act of Charging with Vexing

18.html.title: The Act of Charging with Vexation
18.html.body:
The Act of Charging with Vexation is an act performed against bhikkhus who, when being questioned in the midst of the Community regarding an offense, remain silent and thereby vex the Community.

The text of the Act is as follows:

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu saṅghamajjhe āpattiyā anuyuñjiyamāno tuṇhībhūto saṅghaṃ viheseti. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno vihesakaṃ ropey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu saṅghamajjhe āpattiyā anuyuñjiyamāno tuṇhībhūto saṅghaṃ viheseti. Saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno vihesakaṃ ropeti. Yassāyasmato khamati. (channassa) bhikkhuno vihesakassa ropanā. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati so bhāsey‍ya.

Ropitaṃ saṅghena (channassa) bhikkhuno vihesakaṃ. Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

After this act has been performed, a bhikkhu who vexes the Community by not speaking when questioned about an offense in the midst of the Community incurs a Pācittiya offense.

The Act of Formal Rebuking for bhikkhus holding evil views.

In the time of the Buddha, a bhikkhu named Ariṭṭha held the view: "I understand the Dhamma taught by the Blessed One in such a way that those things called obstructive states by the Blessed One are not actually able to obstruct one who indulges in them." He argued that laypeople who enjoy the five cords of sensual pleasure become Stream-enterers, Once-returners, and Non-returners; and that bhikkhus also attain Arahantship while seeing pleasant forms, hearing sounds, smelling scents, tasting flavors, and experiencing the touch of soft garments. Therefore, he claimed there is no reason why the single act of touching a woman should be obstructive. On account of that matter, the Buddha prescribed the Act of Formal Rebuking for bhikkhus who hold such views and firmly cling to them without abandoning them.

It is performed thus: Bhikkhus who see or hear of a bhikkhu holding such an evil view must admonish him up to three times to abandon that view. If he does not abandon the view upon being admonished, he incurs a Dukkaṭa offense. Bhikkhus who see or hear of it but do not admonish him also incur a Dukkaṭa offense. If he does not abandon the view after being admonished, bring that bhikkhu into the midst of the Community and admonish him up to three times. If he does not comply, he incurs another Dukkaṭa offense. If he still does not abandon it, perform the Act of Formal Rebuking against that bhikkhu.

The text of the Act.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, (tissassa) bhikkhuno evarūpaṃ pāpakaṃ diṭṭhigataṃ uppannaṃ, tathā haṃ bhagavatā dhammaṃ desitaṃ ājānāmi, yathā ye me antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā, te paṭisevato nālaṃ antarāyāyāti. So taṃ diṭṭhiṃ na paṭinissajjati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ samanubhāsey‍ya tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, (tissassa) bhikkhuno evarūpaṃ pāpakaṃ diṭṭhigataṃ uppannaṃ, “Tathāhaṃ bhagavatā dhammaṃ desitaṃ ājānāmi yathā ye me antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṃ antarāyāyāti.” so taṃ diṭṭhiṃ na paṭinissajjati. Saṅgho (tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ samanubhāsati tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya. Yassāyasmato khamati (tissassa) bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. -pe- tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vdāmi.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, (tissassa) bhikkhuno evarūpaṃ pāpakaṃ diṭṭhigataṃ uppannaṃ, tathāhaṃ bhagavato dhammaṃ desitaṃ ājānāmi. Yathā ye me antarāyikā dhammā vuttā bhagavatā te paṭisevato nālaṃ antarāyāyāti. So taṃ diṭṭhiṃ na paṭinissajjati. Saṅgho (tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ samanubhāsati tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya. Yassāyasmato khamati (tissassa) bhikkhuno samanubhāsanā tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Samanubhaṭṭho saṅghena (tisso) bhikkhu tassā diṭṭhiyā paṭinissaggāya. Khamati saṅghassa tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

Immediately upon the conclusion of this act, the bhikkhu holding the view incurs a Pācittiya offense.

The Act of Expulsion for Sāmaṇeras holding wrong views.

If a Sāmaṇera speaks in dispraise of the Buddha, the Dhamma, or the Sangha, or says that what is unallowable is allowable, or holds wrong views, admonish him up to three times to abandon that fault. If the Sāmaṇera does not abandon that view, assemble the Community and tell him in the midst of the Community to abandon that view. If the Sāmaṇera abandons the view, it is well. If he does not abandon it, perform the Act of Expulsion against him. Among the four types of legal acts, this is an Act of Announcement (Apolokana-kamma). When performing it, a competent bhikkhu should announce the expulsion of the Sāmaṇera with the following text:

The text of the Act.

Saṅghaṃ bhante! pucchāmi, ayaṃ (kaṇṭhako) sāmaṇero buddhassa dhammassa saṅghassa avaṇṇavādi micchādiṭṭhiko, yaṃ aññe sāmaṇerā labhanti bhikkhūhi saddhiṃ dvirattatirattaṃ sahasey‍ya, tassā alābhāya nissāraṇā ruccati saṅghassa.

Dutiyampi saṅghaṃ bhante pucchāmi. -pe- nissāraṇā ruccati saṅghassa.

Tatiyampi saṅghaṃ bhante pucchāmi. -pe- nissāraṇā ruccati saṅghassa.

Having thus informed the Community, say to the Sāmaṇera: "Go, unprincipled Sāmaṇera, be destroyed!" A Sāmaṇera who has been thus expelled must be shunned by all bhikkhus. A bhikkhu who supports such an expelled Sāmaṇera, or accepts services from him, or shares Dhamma or material things with him, or sleeps in the same lodging with him, incurs a Pācittiya offense.
18.html.prevLabel: The Act of Charging with Contradiction

19.html.title: Rehabilitation (Osāraṇa)
19.html.body:
If a novice who has been expelled has reformed, abandoned his wrong views, and asks the Community for forgiveness, have him request the removal of the act of expulsion three times. He should then be rehabilitated by an act of announcement (apalokana-kamma).

The Act-Text

Saṅghaṃ bhante! āpucchāmi. Ayaṃ (kaṇṭhako) sāmaṇero buddhassa dhammassa saṅghassa avaṇṇavādī micchādiṭṭhiko. Yaṃ aññe sāmaṇerā labhanti dviratta tirattaṃ bhikkhūhi saddhiṃ sahasey‍yaṃ, tassā alābhāya nissārito. Svāyaṃ idāni orato virato lajjidhammaṃ okkanto, hirottappe patiṭṭhito, katadaṇḍa kammo, accayaṃ deseti. Imassa sāmaṇerassa yathā pure kāyasambhoga sāmaggidānaṃ. Ruccati saṅghassa.

Dutiyampi -pe- tatiyampi -pe-
19.html.prevLabel: The Act of Charging with Harassment

2-1.html.title: Shaving (Bhandukamma)
2-1.html.body:
The first Vinaya procedure that bhikkhus must perform when admitting a faithful clansman—who desires to realize Nibbana—into this noble, emancipating Dispensation established by our Blessed, Worthy, and Rightly Self-Awakened One to release helpless beings wandering in Samsara and burning with the fires of suffering, is called ‘Shaving’ (Bhaṇḍukamma). It is a Vinaya procedure concerning the shaving of the hair of candidates for the Going Forth (pabbajjā).

During the time the Buddha was dwelling [in the world], a certain son of a family quarreled with his parents, fled from home, and went to a monastery. There, he received the Going Forth from a certain Elder. This was done privately, and many bhikkhus were unaware of it. The parents of the child, searching for him, arrived at that monastery and asked the bhikkhus they encountered, “Venerable Sirs, have you seen such a child? Do you know where such a child has gone?” Because those bhikkhus had not seen the child, they said, “We have not seen him,” and because they did not know, they said, “We do not know about that child.”

The parents, not being satisfied with this, searched here and there and eventually saw the child who had gone forth. Becoming angry with the bhikkhus, they reviled them, saying, “These bhikkhus say they have not seen what they have seen, and they say they do not know what they know; they are liars. These lying bhikkhus are shameless (alajjī) and immoral,” and so forth. The bhikkhus reported this matter to the Tathagata. Thereupon, the Blessed One, so that such a thing might not happen in the future, prescribed the Shaving procedure, saying:

“Anujānāmi bhikkhave! saṅghaṃ apalokatuṃ bhaṇḍukammāya” (I allow you, monks, to ask the Community for the shaving.)

This procedure should be performed when the hair of one who has hair longer than two finger-breadths is being cut by a bhikkhu or by the order of a bhikkhu. Perform it in this manner: Assemble all the bhikkhus residing within the monastery and bring the candidate for the Going Forth before the Community. The teacher or another bhikkhu shall then announce before the Community:

“Saṅghaṃ bhante! imassa dārakassa bhaṇḍukammaṃ āpucchāmi” (Venerable Sirs, I ask the Community for the shaving of this boy.) Recite this sentence once, twice, or thrice. For the Shaving, it is also proper to say “Imassa dārakassa bhaṇḍukammaṃ āpucchāmi” or “Imassa samaṇa karaṇaṃ āpucchāmi” or “Ayaṃ samaṇo hotu kāmo”.

Without assembling the bhikkhus, it is also proper to go to the places where the individual bhikkhus reside and inquire regarding the Shaving. The preceptor himself need not go to the bhikkhus; it is also proper to send another to inquire from them. If the procedure must be performed at a time when bhikkhus are so numerous that it is difficult to assemble them all or to inform them individually, go to a formal boundary (baddha-sīmā) or a water-boundary (udakukkhepa-sīmā) and perform the procedure in the presence of the bhikkhus assembled there.

The shaving of the head of one who is to go forth is a significant matter. If the person going forth is one of merit, virtue, and wisdom, who can be of special benefit to the Sasana in the future, the teachers should take special care regarding such individuals. When ordaining such a person, the best method is for the teacher himself to take the candidate to the place of water and shave the head, rather than sending the candidate away to shave and bathe alone.

The moment of going forth is a time when a clansman's mind is very clear and confident. Therefore, what is taught at that time is deeply impressed upon his mind. The occasion of shaving the head is the most opportune time to instruct the candidate in the ‘Meditation on the Fivefold Skin-parts’ (taca-pañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna). Certain clansmen with the requisite supporting conditions (upanissaya-sampatti), by reflecting on the taca-pañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna taught at this moment, directing the mind accordingly, defining mind and matter (nāma-rūpa), and seeing the three characteristics of impermanence etc. in formations, realize the Four Noble Truths and attain Arahantship through the stages of the Path and Fruit even while the hair is being shaved, destroying all defilements and realizing Nibbana. There are many mentions in our texts of individuals who became Arahants while their hair was being shaved.

Even for clansmen who do not have the supporting conditions to become Arahants at that very moment, what is taught then is of great benefit as it is deeply impressed upon the mind. To ensure the candidate benefits from this occasion, the teacher should shave the head while instructing him in the taca-pañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna. If the teacher is unable to shave the head and has another do it, the teacher should stand nearby and instruct the candidate in the taca-pañcaka-kammaṭṭhāna. Alternatively, the teacher should appoint another bhikkhu to do so. In doing this, first place the lock of hair that is cut into the hand of the candidate, tell him to look at it well and take its nature to heart, and then instruct him in the meditation. Instruct him as follows:

2-2.html.title: The Meditation on the Fivefold Skin-Constituents
2-2.html.body:
1. These hairs of the head, being a part of the body: are loathsome because of their foul smell, because they originate from impurities such as bile, phlegm, blood, pus, feces, and urine, and because they arise within the heap of thirty-one impurities.
  2. These hairs of the body, being a part of the body: are loathsome because of their foul smell, because they originate from impurities such as bile, phlegm, blood, pus, feces, and urine, and because they arise within the heap of thirty-one impurities.
  3. These nails, being a part of the body: are loathsome because of their foul smell, because they originate from impurities such as bile, phlegm, blood, pus, feces, and urine, and because they arise within the heap of thirty-one impurities.
  4. These teeth, being a part of the body: are loathsome because of their foul smell, because they originate from impurities such as bile, phlegm, blood, pus, feces, and urine, and because they arise within the heap of thirty-one impurities.
  5. This skin, being a part of the body: is loathsome because of its foul smell, because it originates from impurities such as bile, phlegm, blood, pus, feces, and urine, and because it arises within the heap of thirty-one impurities.

By way of elements:

  1. These hairs of the head, being a part of the body: are a portion of the earth element (paṭhavī-dhātu), which is not a living being, not a person, not a self, and has no owner.
  2. These hairs of the body, being a part of the body: are a portion of the earth element (paṭhavī-dhātu), which is not a living being, not a person, not a self, and has no owner.
  3. These nails, being a part of the body: are a portion of the earth element (paṭhavī-dhātu), which is not a living being, not a person, not a self, and has no owner.
  4. These teeth, being a part of the body: are a portion of the earth element (paṭhavī-dhātu), which is not a living being, not a person, not a self, and has no owner.
  5. This skin, being a part of the body: is a portion of the earth element (paṭhavī-dhātu), which is not a living being, not a person, not a self, and has no owner.

The candidate for ordination should continue this meditation without neglect even after going forth.

After the hair is shaved, the candidate must bathe and cleanse himself thoroughly with soap to remove the scent of the laity. If the candidate is unable to do so himself, you, as the teacher, must bathe and cleanse the candidate for going forth (pabbajjāpekkha). In doing so, act out of compassion, without feeling disgust, just as a father would bathe a beloved son. Thereafter, having dressed him in clean clothes, lead the candidate to the place of ordination. Upon arrival, if the parents of the clansman (kulaputta) are present, he should first pay homage to their feet and take leave of them. Then, having paid homage to the feet of the preceptor (upajjhāya) and the assembled bhikkhus, he should take the robes in his hands, sit in the squatting position (ukkutika) before the preceptor, and with hands in añjali, request the going forth. The formula for this is as follows:

Okāsa! Ahaṃ bhante! Pabbajjaṃ yācāmi.

Dutiyampi ahaṃ bhante! Pabbajjaṃ yācāmi.

Tatiyampi ahaṃ bhante! Pabbajjaṃ yācāmi.

Sabba dukkha nissaraṇa nibbāna sacchikaraṇatthāya imaṃ kāsāvaṃ gahetvā pabbājetha maṃ bhante! Anukampaṃ upādāya.

Dutiyampi sabba dukkha nissaraṇa nibbāna sacchikaraṇatthāya imaṃ kāsāvaṃ gahetvā pabbājetha maṃ bhante! Anukampaṃ upādāya.

Tatiyampi sabba dukkha nissaraṇa nibbāna sacchikaraṇatthāya imaṃ kāsāvaṃ gahetvā pabbājetha maṃ bhante! Anukampaṃ upādāya.

Having recited this formula and offered his robes into the hands of the preceptor, he should again sit in the squatting position before the preceptor and, with hands in añjali, recite this formula:

Sabba dukkha nissaraṇa nibbāna sacchikaraṇatthāya etaṃ kāsāvaṃ datvā pabbājetha maṃ bhante anukampaṃ upādāya. Dutiyampi sabba dukkha -pe- upādāya. Tatiyampi sabba dukkha -pe- upādāya.

When the candidate has requested the robes with this formula, you, as the teacher, should place the shoulder-strap (aṃsa) around the candidate's neck and give him the robes you hold for the purpose of dressing and robing. The candidate should then reflect upon the robes received from the teacher and put them on. If the candidate is unable to dress himself, the teacher or another should assist him. One should not put on the robes as a layman without receiving them from a teacher. If the candidate is already wearing robes, they must be removed and then given back to him for the ordination. If the Refuges are undertaken without doing so, the going forth is not established. It is also permissible for another to dress him upon the preceptor's command. If the preceptor gives the robes to a layman saying, "Dress him in these robes," it is permissible for the layman to do so by the preceptor's command. After being robed, the candidate should return to the preceptor, pay homage to his feet with the fivefold prostration, sit in the squatting position with hands in añjali, and request the ten precepts of going forth along with the Triple Refuge using this formula:

Okāsa! Ahaṃ bhante tisaraṇena saddhiṃ pabbajjā dasasīlaṃ dhammaṃ yācāmi: anuggahaṃ katvā sīlaṃ detha me bhante. Dutiyampi ahaṃ bhante -pe- me bhante. Tatiyampi ahaṃ bhante -pe- me bhante anukampaṃ upādāya.

When this has been said, the preceptor should say, “Yamahaṃ vadāmi taṃ vadehi” and have him recite the Namaskāra, and then:

Buddhaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi.

Dhammaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi.

Saṅghaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi.

.

Dutiyampi Buddhaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi.

Dutiyampi Dhammaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi.

Dutiyampi Saṅghaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi.

.

Tatiyampi Buddhaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi.

Tatiyampi Dhammaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi.

Tatiyampi Saṅghaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi.

The Refuges should be undertaken either by joining the words with the niggahita ending (e.g., “Buddhaṃ saraṇaṃ gacchāmi”) or by separating the words with the 'm' ending (e.g., “Buddham saraṇam gacchāmi”). It is better to have them undertaken in both ways. Many teachers do so. Even if the training rules such as “Pāṇātipātā veramaṇī sikkhāpadaṃ samādiyāmi” are not recited, the status of a novice (sāmaṇera) is attained through the undertaking of the Refuges alone. The recitation of the training rules is not the primary factor here.

Since the intention (cetanā) is the primary factor in a layman's taking of refuge, the taking of refuge occurs regardless of the language or manner in which the training rules are recited. However, for a novice, the taking of refuge occurs only when the teacher recites the words in the Pali language without error in pronunciation or articulation (ṭhāna-karaṇa-sampatti), and the pupil likewise recites them without error. If even one of the two—teacher or pupil—is unable to pronounce correctly, the taking of refuge does not occur. Elderly persons without teeth cannot pronounce them perfectly. Therefore, when an elderly thera without teeth ordains a pupil, he should have another person administer the Refuges.

2-3.html.title: Taking a Preceptor
2-3.html.body:
After undertaking the novice virtue, the one who has received the going-forth should pay homage to the Preceptor, sit in the squatting position, and take the Preceptor by reciting three times the phrase: “Upajjhāyo me bhante hohi”. When the pupil has spoken thus, the Preceptor should accept it by saying: “Pāsādikena sampādehi”.

According to the word of the Buddha: “Upajjhāyo bhikkhave! saddhivihārikamhi putta cittaṃ upaṭṭhapessati. Saddhivihāriko upajjhāyamhi pitūcittaṃ upaṭṭhapessati. Evaṃ te aññamaññaṃ sagāravā sappatissā sabhāgavuttikā viharantā imasmiṃ dhammavinaye vuddhiṃ virūḷhiṃ vepullaṃ āpajjanti”, the novice should regard the Preceptor as his father, and with great respect and reverence for the Preceptor, live in complete obedience to him. The Preceptor should also regard his co-resident novice as a son and be compassionate toward him. The Preceptor should train the new novice in wearing the robes and carrying the bowl, etc., by having him reside in his presence. Until these are mastered, he should protect the new novice like one protects an infant, without sending him on invitations and such. He should teach the training rules that a novice must observe. The novice also—

“Anujānāmi bhikkhave! dasabhaṅgehi samannāgataṃ sāmaṇeraṃ nāsetuṃ, pāṇātipāti hoti. Adinnādāyi hoti, ab‍rahmacārī hoti. Musāvādi hoti, majjapāyi hoti, buddhassa avaṇṇaṃ bhāsati, dhammassa avaṇṇaṃ bhāsati, saṅghassa avaṇṇaṃ bhāsati, micchādiṭṭhiko hoti, bhikkhunī dusako hoti”

—should live obediently without breaking even one of these ten factors for expulsion mentioned above. If even one of these is violated, the entire novice virtue, beginning from the going for refuge, is broken. The taking of the Preceptor is also nullified. He loses his right to the lodging he received in the monastery and to the gains of the Community. If he does not establish himself in future restraint and take the refuges again, that novice should be expelled by having his robes removed and being driven out.

If a novice who has committed a defeat acknowledges his fault and wishes to be established in restraint again, he should be made to take the refuges again and take a Preceptor again. The going for refuge of novices is like the formal act of Higher Ordination (upasampadā-kammavācā) for bhikkhus. Just as the undertaking of the fourfold purification of virtue occurs for a bhikkhu through the recitation of the ordination kammavācā, the undertaking of the ten precepts for novices occurs through the going for refuge itself. However, for the sake of establishing future restraint and for the sake of firming the commitment, after the going for refuge, he should also be made to undertake the ten training rules. If a novice commits a fault that does not belong to the ten factors for expulsion, some form of punishment such as the water-punishment or sand-punishment should be imposed. By undergoing the punishment and being established in future restraint, his virtue is purified. There is no fault in not undertaking the training rules again.
2-3.html.prevLabel: The Meditation on the Fivefold Skin-Pentad

2-4.html.title: Those Unfit for Going-forth
2-4.html.body:
There are two classes of persons unfit for Going-forth: those who are excluded from Going-forth such that even if the ceremony is performed, the status is not attained; and those for whom Going-forth is prohibited because it is unseemly, though the status would be attained if the ceremony were performed. A teacher incurs an offense of wrongdoing (dukkaṭa) by granting Going-forth to those who are unfit.

There are eleven persons excluded from Going-forth:

  1. Paṇḍako bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.
  2. Ubhatobyañjanako bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.
  3. Theyyasaṃvāsako bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.
  4. Titthiyapakkantako bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.
  5. Tiracchānagato bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.
  6. Mātughātako bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.
  7. Pitughātako bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.
  8. Arahantaghātako bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.
  9. Lohituppādako bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.
  10. Saṅghabhedako bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.
  11. Bhikkhunīdūsako bhikkhave! anupasampanno na upasampādetabbo, upasampanno nāsetabbo.

The eleven persons mentioned in these eleven disciplinary rules—the paṇḍaka, ubhatobyañjanaka, theyyasaṃvāsaka, titthiyapakkantaka, tiracchānagata, mātughātaka, pitughātaka, arahantaghātaka, lohituppādaka, saṅghabhedaka, and bhikkhunīdūsaka—are excluded from both Going-forth and Higher Ordination. Even if they are given the Going-forth, the status is not established. Even if they are given the Higher Ordination, the status is not established.

Paṇḍaka refers to persons with a deficiency in gender characteristics. There are five types of paṇḍaka: the āsittapaṇḍaka, the usūyapaṇḍaka, the opakkamikapaṇḍaka, the pakkhapaṇḍaka, and the napuṃsakapaṇḍaka. One who calms his sexual fever by taking another's genitals in his mouth and sucking the impurity is called an āsittapaṇḍaka. One who calms his sexual fever by watching others engage in sexual intercourse is called an usūyapaṇḍaka. One who has been castrated, as is done to dogs or cattle, is called an opakkamikapaṇḍaka. One who, through the power of unwholesome kamma, becomes a paṇḍaka during the waning half of the month is called a pakkhapaṇḍaka. One who is born without either male or female gender characteristics is called a napuṃsakapaṇḍaka. Among these five types of paṇḍaka, Going-forth is not prohibited for the āsitta and usūya paṇḍaka. The remaining three are unfit for Going-forth. Even for the pakkhapaṇḍaka, Going-forth is prohibited only during the period he remains a paṇḍaka. During that time, he becomes crazed with sexual fever. The sexual fever of the āsitta and usūya paṇḍaka can be calmed through wise reflection (yoniso manasikāra) and other means. The sexual fever of the pakkhapaṇḍaka cannot be so calmed. That is why he is unfit for Going-forth during that period.

Ubhatobyañjanaka refers to a person in whom both male and female characteristics are present. There are two types: the female ubhatobyañjanaka and the male ubhatobyañjanaka. Among them, the female ubhatobyañjanaka is capable of both conceiving and causing other women to conceive. The male ubhatobyañjanaka can cause women to conceive but cannot conceive himself. Both are capable of performing both male and female roles during sexual gratification.

There are three types of theyyasaṃvāsaka: the liṅgatthenaka, the saṃvāsatthenaka, and the ubhayatthenaka. One who, without receiving Going-forth from a teacher, dons the robes himself, does not declare his seniority in rains, does not accept salutation according to seniority, does not obstruct other bhikkhus by taking a seat, and does not participate in formal acts of the Community (vinaya kamma), but merely steals the outward appearance of a recluse, is called a liṅgatthenaka. One who has received only the novice Going-forth from bhikkhus but goes to another region and falsely declares, "I have this many rains," accepts salutations from bhikkhus according to seniority, obstructs bhikkhus by taking a seat, and presents himself at formal acts of the Community, is called a saṃvāsatthenaka because he steals only the communion. One who dons the robes himself, goes to a monastery, declares a number of rains, accepts salutations from bhikkhus, obstructs bhikkhus by taking a seat, and participates in formal acts of the Community, is called an ubhayatthenaka. A detailed explanation of the theyyasaṃvāsaka is found in the Samantapāsādikā, which should be consulted.

Titthiyapakkantaka refers to one who, having been ordained as a bhikkhu in the Buddha's Dispensation, later takes up the going-forth of another sect and adopts their views.

Tiracchānagata here refers not only to animals but to all non-human beings such as nāga, supaṇṇa, deva, and brahmā. Going-forth is a privilege reserved for human beings alone.

Mātughātaka refers to one who has committed the act of matricide, which is one of the weightier kammas with immediate result (ānantariya kamma). This kamma occurs when a child of the human race intentionally takes the life of a mother who is also of the human race.

The same applies to the pitughātaka (patricide). Even if one does not know that the person being killed is one's father, the ānantariya kamma is committed by the act of killing. Details should be consulted in the Samantapāsādikā. An explanation of these kammas is also found in the 'Handbook of the Buddhist'.

Arahantaghātaka should also be understood in terms of human Arahants. If a human intentionally takes the life of an Arahant of the human race, whether ordained or not, it is an ānantariya kamma. He cannot receive Going-forth. If one kills an Arahant who is not human, it is only a grave sin, not an ānantariya kamma. If an animal takes the life of a human Arahant, the ānantariya kamma does not occur; it is only a grave sin.

Lohituppādaka refers to one who, like Devadatta, with a malicious mind, causes even a single drop of blood to be shed from the body of a living Buddha. For him, Going-forth and Higher Ordination are prohibited. For one who draws blood from the Buddha's body with a mind of loving-kindness in order to cure an illness, there is merit and no sin.

Saṅghabhedaka refers to one who, like Devadatta, causes a schism in the Community by means of any of the four types of formal acts. This is a kamma that can only be committed by one who has received Higher Ordination, not by one who has not.

Bhikkhunīdūsaka refers to one who has violated a regular bhikkhuni in any of the three passages. For him, Going-forth and Higher Ordination are prohibited. For one who has merely compromised the virtue of a bhikkhuni through bodily contact, Going-forth and Higher Ordination are not prohibited.
2-4.html.nextLabel: Persons for whom Going-forth is Prohibited but Valid if Performed.

2-5.html.title: Persons who should not be ordained, but whose ordination is valid if performed.
2-5.html.body:
“Na bhikkhave! ananuññāto mātā pitūhi putto pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa” Since this is a Vinaya regulation, do not ordain a son who has not been permitted by his mother and father. Even if one who was previously ordained with parental permission has left the robes and returns to be ordained again, do not ordain him without obtaining parental permission once more. If parents have entrusted a child saying, “I entrust him to you; ordain him whenever you wish,” then you may ordain that child whenever he comes, even if he has left the robes many times, without further consulting the parents. If someone comes to the monastery after a quarrel with his parents and asks for ordination, and when told to bring parental permission, says, “I will not go to my parents; if you do not ordain me, I will take my life or cause harm to you,” then ordain him for the sake of his protection. If his parents come and inquire about such a person who has been ordained, inform them of the circumstances.

“Na bhikkhave! pañcahi ābādhehi puṭṭho pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa” Since this is a Vinaya regulation, do not ordain anyone afflicted with any of the five diseases. The five diseases are those known as Kuṭṭha, gaṇḍa, kilāsa, sosa, apamāra.

‘Kuṭṭha’ (Leprosy) refers to diseases arising on the skin such as red spots, black spots, and scabs. Do not ordain one who has leprosy even the size of a little finger-nail if it is in the spreading stage. It is suitable to ordain one who has a small patch of leprosy only the size of a nail on a part of the body covered by the robe, provided it is in the healing stage. When ordaining a leper after treatment, ordain him only when the skin has returned to its natural color. The Vinaya Commentary states that one whose body is covered in ash-like scales like the back of a monitor lizard should also not be ordained.

‘Gaṇḍa’ (Boils) refers to unripened lumps arising here and there on the body like ebony fruits or limes. Some call these cystic tumors. Do not ordain one who has a growing boil even the size of a jujube fruit. One who has a non-growing boil the size of a jujube fruit in a covered area may be ordained. Do not ordain one who has non-growing boils on uncovered areas like the face. When ordaining after treating boils, ordain only after the skin has returned to normal. Do not ordain while the wound is still present.

‘Kilāsa’ (Eczema/Dry Leprosy) is a specific type of dry skin disease appearing in the color of red or white lotus petals. The condition commonly known today as vitiligo is Kilāsa. Regarding those with this disease, follow the same procedure as mentioned for leprosy.

‘Sosa’ (Consumption) is tuberculosis, which gradually wastes and dries the body day by day, leading the patient to death.

‘Apamāra’ (Epilepsy) is the falling sickness where a person suddenly loses consciousness and falls to the ground, sometimes struggling and foaming at the mouth with phlegm and blood. It is said to be a disease caused by non-human spirits.

“Na bhikkhave! rājabhaṭo pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya, āpatti dukkaṭassa” Since this is a Vinaya regulation, do not ordain a king’s soldier (government servant). A ‘king’s soldier’ is one who receives a salary from a king or a government for performing any task. It is proper to ordain such persons after they have resigned from their service.

“Na bhikkhave! dhajabandho coro pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa” As declared, do not ordain a notorious thief who commits serious robberies. One who was previously a notorious thief but has since abandoned that path and entered the right way, and is known as such by the public, is suitable for ordination. One who has committed minor thefts but is not publicly known as a thief may be given ordination.

“Na bhikkhave! kārabhedako coro pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa” Since these are Vinaya rules, do not ordain a thief who has escaped from confinement. This includes one who has escaped from a prison, one who has escaped from guards while being moved from place to place, and one who has fled an area after being restricted to it; all such persons are unfit for ordination. If one who was living righteously was imprisoned due to the slander of others and escaped, do not ordain him in that same region. He may be ordained in another region.

“Na bhikkhave! likhitako coro pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa” Since these are Vinaya rules, do not ordain a thief whose name is recorded by the government for arrest or punishment.

“Na bhikkhave! kasāhato katadaṇḍakammo pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa” Since these are Vinaya rules, do not ordain one who has been flogged with a whip or other instrument as punishment. One who was beaten for failing to follow instructions may be ordained. Those who should not be ordained are those flogged as punishment for non-payment of taxes, non-payment of debts, etc. Even such a person is fit to receive ordination after the wounds have healed.

“Na bhikkhave! lakkhaṇāhato katadaṇḍakammo pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa” Since these are Vinaya rules, do not ordain one who has been branded or marked on the body as punishment. If the branded person is a free man, he may be ordained after the wounds have healed and the scars have faded. One who has scars in places not visible when the robes are worn covering the three circles may also be ordained.

“Na bhikkhave! iṇāyiko pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa” As declared, do not ordain debtors. If a relative or a lay supporter of the person seeking ordination undertakes to pay the debt, he may be ordained. If the preceptor has funds to pay the debt, he may ordain the debtor with the intention of freeing him by paying the creditors. Do not ordain with the thought of finding money to pay the debt later. There is no offense for the preceptor if he ordains a person without knowing he is a debtor.

“Na bhikkhave! dāso pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa” As declared, do not ordain a slave. Since there are no slaves at present, no detailed explanation is provided here. There is an extensive explanation regarding this in the Commentary.

Thirty-two types of persons who should not be ordained.

“Na bhikkhave! hatthacchinno pabbājetabbo. Na pādacchinno pabbājetabbo. Na hatthapādacchinno pabbājetabbo. Na kaṇṇacchinno pabbājetabbo. Na kāsacchinno pabbājetabbo. Nakaṇṇanāsacchinno pabbājetabbo. Na aṅgulicchinno pabbājetabbo. Na alacchinno pabbājetabbo. Na kaṇḍaracchinno pabbājetabbo. Na phaṇahattho pabbājetabbo. Na khujjo pabbājetabbo. Na vāmano pabbājetabbo. Na galagaṇḍi pabbājetabbo. Na lakkhaṇāhato pabbājetabbo. Na kasāhato pabbājetabbo. Na likhitako pabbājetabbo. Na sīpadi pabbājetabbo. Na pāparogi pabbājetabbo. Na parisadūsako pabbājetabbo. Na kāṇo pabbājetabbo. Na kuṇi pabbājetabbo. Na bañjo pabbājetabbo. Na pakkhahato pabbājetabbo. Na chinniriyāpatho pabbājetabbo. Na jarādubbalo pabbājetabbo. Na andho pabbājetabbo. Na mūgo pabbājetabbo. Na badhiro pabbājetabbo. Na andhamūgo pabbājetabbo. Na andhabadhiro pabbājetabbo. Na mugabadhiro pabbājetabbo. Na andhamugabadhiro pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa.

As the Tathāgata has declared in the Vinaya, the following should not be ordained: one whose hands are cut off, one whose feet are cut off, one whose hands and feet are cut off, one whose ears are cut off, one whose nose is cut off, one whose ears and nose are cut off, one whose thumbs are cut off, one whose little fingers are cut off, one with severed tendons, one with webbed fingers, a hunchback, a dwarf, one with a goiter, one with branded marks on the body, one with marks of flogging, one who is a wanted criminal, one with elephantiasis, one with a bad disease, one who disfigures the assembly, one who is one-eyed, one with a crooked limb, one who is lame, one who is paralyzed on one side, a cripple, one feeble with age, one who is blind, one who is dumb, one who is deaf, one who is blind and dumb, one who is blind and deaf, one who is dumb and deaf, and one who is blind, dumb, and deaf. If ordained, there is an offense of wrong-doing for the preceptor.

One whose finger-tips only are cut off such that even a small portion of the nail remains may be ordained. If even one finger is cut off such that no nail remains, he should not be ordained.

‘One with severed tendons’ refers to one who, due to severed tendons, cannot place the entire sole of the foot on the ground and walks only on the tips of the toes or only on the heels.

‘One with webbed fingers’ refers to one whose fingers are joined together.

‘One with a bad disease’ refers to one suffering from diseases such as asthma, hemorrhoids, fistula, or chronic cough, which are loathed by the public.

‘One who disfigures the assembly’ refers to one who, due to deformity, makes the assembly look unsightly. This includes: extremely tall persons whose heads reach the waist of others; extremely short persons like monstrous images; extremely dark persons like a charred log; extremely pale persons like a plastered wall; extremely thin persons like hungry ghosts (petas) with only skin and bone; extremely obese persons who find it difficult to move; those with massive bellies like goblins (pisacas); and those with heads so large they cannot be held up. A detailed description of those who disfigure the assembly is found in the Vinaya Commentary, where many such types are listed.

2-6.html.title: The Age for Ordination
2-6.html.body:
“Na bhikkhave! ūnapaṇṇarasavasso dārako pabbājetabbo. Yo pabbājey‍ya āpatti dukkaṭassa.” Regarding this, the Blessed One once declared that a boy under fifteen years of age should not be given the Going Forth, and that whoever should give the Going Forth to such a one commits an offense of wrong-doing.

In later times, a very devout family who were attendants to the Venerable Ānanda Thera passed away due to the plague (ahivātaka disease). Two young children of that family survived. When they saw the bhikkhus, they would run towards them out of past familiarity. The bhikkhus would drive them away. Receiving no compassion from the bhikkhus, those children would cry. The heart of the Venerable Ānanda Thera was moved by their suffering, and thinking, “The Buddha has laid down a training rule that children under fifteen years of age should not be ordained; what can be done so that these children do not perish?” he reported the matter to the Buddha.

Then the Tathāgata asked, “Ānanda, are those children capable of scaring away crows?” When he replied, “Yes, Venerable Sir,” the Buddha addressed the bhikkhus and said: “Anujānāmi bhikkhave! ūnapaṇṇarasavassaṃ kākuḍḍepakaṃ pabbājetujaṃ”—thus allowing the Going Forth for children who have not reached fifteen years of age but are capable of scaring away crows. Although the Buddha allowed the ordination of children capable of scaring away crows to prevent those two boys from perishing in that emergency, it should be understood as the Buddha's intention that children under fifteen years of age are generally unsuitable for the Going Forth. Therefore, except for a special reason, children under fifteen years of age should not be ordained. The period between fifteen and twenty years of age should be considered the most suitable time for ordaining young men. For those who ordain late in life, it is difficult to discipline the mind and body to be suitable for the life of a renunciant. Therefore, the Buddha declared:

“Pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato dullabho vuḍḍhapabbajito. Katamehi pañcahi? Dullabho bhikkhave! buḍḍhapabbajito suvaco. Dullabho suggahitagāhī. Dullabho padakkhiṇaggāhī. Dullabho dhammakathiko. Dullabho vinayadharo.”

This means that a late-life ordinee possessed of five qualities is rare. “Bhikkhus, a late-life ordinee possessed of these five qualities is rare. Which five? Bhikkhus, a late-life ordinee who is easy to speak to is rare; a late-life ordinee who learns well from teachers is rare; a late-life ordinee who accepts the instructions of teachers respectfully is rare; a late-life ordinee who is a speaker of Dhamma is rare; a late-life ordinee who is a bearer of the Vinaya is rare.” In another Sutta, the Buddha also declared: “Dullabho ākappasampanno”—that a late-life ordinee accomplished in proper conduct and deportment is rare.

Wealth, education, or noble birth do not appear in the Buddha's teaching as factors to be considered when determining suitability or unsuitability for ordination. If one is free from the aforementioned disqualifications for the Going Forth, anyone—whether rich or poor, educated or uneducated—is suitable for ordination.

“Yebhuy‍yena hi kattiyakulato pabbajitā jātiṃ nissāya mānaṃ karonti. B‍rāhmaṇakulā pabbajitā mante nissāya mānaṃ karonti. Hīnajaccakulā pabbajitā attano vijātitāya patiṭṭhātuṃ na sakkonti. Gahapatidārakā pana kacchehi sedaṃ muñcantehi piṭṭhiyā loṇaṃ pupphamānāya bhūmiṃ kasitvā nihatamānadappā honti. Te pabbajitvā mānaṃ vā dappaṃ vā akatvā buddhavacanaṃ uggahetvā vipassanāya kammaṃ karontā sakkonti arahatte patiṭṭhātuṃ.”

This passage from the Commentary to the Cūḷahatthipadopama Sutta should also be considered in this regard.
