20.html.title: The Higher Penalty (Brahmadaṇḍa)
20.html.body:
The Tathāgata prescribed the Higher Penalty for the bhikkhu named Channa, who behaved by reviling the bhikkhus and refusing to accept the exhortations and instructions of his fellow celibates. The Vinaya Commentary states: “Yo añño’pi bhikkhu mukharo hoti bhikkhuṃ aruttavacanehi ghaṭṭento khuṃsento vamhento viharati tassā’pi dātabbo” — meaning this penalty should also be imposed upon any other harsh-spoken bhikkhu who dwells harassing, disparaging, and scorning bhikkhus with improper speech. This penalty must be imposed by an Act of Proclamation (apalokana-kamma).

The Formal Act (Kammavācā).

Bhante (it‍thaṃ nāmo) bhikkhu mukharo, bhikkhu duruttavacanehi ghaṭṭento viharati. So bhikkhu yaṃ icchey‍ya taṃ vadey‍ya. Bhikkhūhi (it‍thaṃ nāmo) bhikkhu neva vattabbo, na ovaditabbo, na anusāsitabbo. Saṅghaṃ bhante pucchāmi (itthannāmassa) bhikkhuno b‍rahmadaṇḍassa dānaṃ. Ruccati saṅghassa.

Dutiyampi -pe- tatiyampi -pe-

After this act is performed, whatever that bhikkhu may say, the other bhikkhus shall say nothing to him. He shall not be exhorted nor instructed. If that bhikkhu later becomes disciplined, abandons his evil ways, and asks for forgiveness from the Community, the penalty shall be revoked by the Community.

The Formal Act for Revocation.

Bhante! bhikkhusaṅgho (asukassa) bhikkhuno b‍rahmadaṇḍaṃ adāsi. So bhikkhu sorato nivātavutti lajjidhammaṃ okkanto, hirottappe patiṭṭhito, paṭisaṅkhā āyatiṃ saṃvare tiṭṭhati. Saṅghaṃ bhante! pucchāmi tassa bhikkhuno b‍rahmadaṇḍassa paṭippassaddhiṃ. Ruccati saṅghassa.

Dutiyampi -pe- tatiyampi -pe-
20.html.nextLabel: The Act of Charging with Delusion

21.html.title: The Act of Charging with Delusion
21.html.body:
Certain bhikkhus, when the Patimokkha is being recited, do not listen attentively and subsequently commit an offense, saying: "We did not know earlier that this was a fault—that there is an offense for this; we have only now learned that such a training rule exists." They thus attempt to convince the Community that the offense was committed through ignorance. The Act of Charging with Delusion is the formal act prescribed for the purpose of disciplining such bhikkhus.

Ignorance of a training rule is not a valid ground for exemption from an offense. Therefore, have the bhikkhu who claims to have committed the offense through ignorance confess that offense. If the bhikkhus are aware that the offending bhikkhu has attended the recitation of the Patimokkha at least two or three times previously, the Community should carry out the Act of Charging with Delusion against him.

The formal act-statement for performing it is as follows:

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu pātimokkhe uddissamāne na sādhukaṃ aṭṭikat‍vā manasikaroti. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno mohaṃ āropey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (tisso) bhikkhu pātimokkhe uddissamāne na sādhukaṃ aṭṭikat‍vā manasikaroti. Saṅgho (tissassa) bhikkhuno mohaṃ āropeti. Yassāyasmato khamati. (tissassa) bhikkhuno mohassa āropanā. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati so bhāsey‍ya.

Āropito saṅghena (tissassa) bhikkhuno moho. Khamati saṅghassa. Tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

If, after this formal act has been performed, the bhikkhu still claims ignorance of the training rule, he incurs a Pacittiya offense.

22.html.title: The Act of Censure (Tajjanīya Kamma)
22.html.body:
During the time of the Buddha, the two bhikkhus named Paṇḍuka and Lohitaka were frequently makers of strife, makers of quarrels, makers of disputes, makers of idle talk, and makers of legal questions in the Community, and they exhorted other bhikkhus to do the same. To discipline such bhikkhus, the Blessed One prescribed this Act of Censure (Tajjanīya Kamma). When performing it, bring the quarrelsome bhikkhus into the midst of the Community, charge them, remind them of the offense, and having made them acknowledge the offense, the formal act should be performed.

The formal text of the motion is as follows:

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ime (Paṇḍuka Lohitakā) bhikkhū attanā bhaṇḍanakārakā kalahakārakā vivādakārakā bhassakārakā saṅghe adhikaraṇakārakā, yepi caññe bhikkhū bhaṇḍanakārakā kalahakārakā vivādakārakā bhassakārakā saṅghe adhikaraṇakārakā. Te upasaṅkamitvā evaṃ vadanti “mā kho tumhe āyasmanto eso ajesi, balavā balavaṃ paṭimantetha, tumhe tena paṇḍitatarā ca byattatarā ca bahussutatarā ca alamattatarā ca, mā cassa bhāyittha. Mayampi tumhākaṃ pakkhā bhavissāmā”ti. Tena anuppannāni ceva bhaṇḍanāni uppajjanti uppannāni ca bhaṇḍanāni bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṃvattanti. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīyakammaṃ kareyya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ime (Paṇḍuka Lohitakā) bhikkhū attanā bhaṇḍana kārakā -pe- saṅgho adhikaraṇa kārakā, yepi caññe bhikkhū bhaṇḍanakārakā -pe- saṅghe adhikaraṇakārakā, te upasaṅkamitvā evaṃ vadanti “mā kho tumhe āyasmanto eso ajesi, balavā balavaṃ paṭimantetha tumhe tena paṇḍitatarā ca byattatarā ca bahussutatarā ca alamattatarā ca mā cassa bhāyittha, mayampi tumhākaṃ pakkhā bhavissāmā” ti tena anuppannāni ca bhaṇḍanāni uppajjanti. Uppannāni ca bhaṇḍanāni bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṃvattanti. Saṅgho (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīya kammaṃ karoti. Yassāyasmato khamati (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīya kammassa karaṇaṃ. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati. So bhāseyya.

Dutiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ime (Paṇḍuka Lohitakā) bhikkhū attanā bhaṇḍanakārakā -pe- saṅghe adhikaraṇakārakā. Ye’pi caññe bhikkhū bhaṇḍanakārakā -pe- saṅghe adhikaraṇakārakā, te upasaṅkamitvā evaṃ vadanti balavā balavaṃ paṭimantetha, tumhe tena paṇḍitatarā ca byattatarā ca bahussutatarā ca alamattatarā ca, mā cassa bhāyittha, mayampi tumhākaṃ pakkhā bhavissāmā”ti. Tena anuppannāni ceva bhaṇḍanāni uppajjanti. Uppannāni ca bhaṇḍanāni bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṃvattanti. Saṅgho (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīyakammaṃ karoti yassāyasmato khamati (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīya kammassa karaṇaṃ. So tuṇhassa, yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya.

Tatiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ime (Paṇḍuka Lohitakā) bhikkhū attanā bhaṇḍanakārakā -pe- saṅghe adhikaraṇakārakā, ye’pi caññe bhikkhū bhaṇḍanakārakā -pe- saṅghe adhikaraṇakārakā te upasaṅkamitvā evaṃ vadanti “mā kho tumhe āyasmanto eso ajesi, balavā balavaṃ paṭimantetha tumhe tena paṇḍitatarā ca byattatarā ca bahussutatarā ca alamattatarā ca. Mā cassa bhāyittha, mayampi tumhākaṃ pakkhā bhavissāmā”ti tena anuppannāni ceva bhaṇḍanāni uppajjanti. Uppannāni ca bhaṇḍanāni bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṃvattanti. Saṅgho (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīya kammaṃ karoti. Yassāyasmato khamati. (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīyakammassa karaṇaṃ, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati. So bhāseyya.

Kataṃ saṅghena (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīya kammaṃ. Khamati saṅghassa. Tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

The Eighteen Duties to be Observed by Bhikkhus Subjected to the Act of Censure

A bhikkhu who has been subjected to the Act of Censure:

  1. Should not confer the full admission (Upasampadā) as a Preceptor or as a Teacher.
  2. Should not give the going-forth (Pabbajja) to a youth as a Preceptor.
  3. Should not give dependence (Nissaya) to other bhikkhus.
  4. Should not accept the agreement to exhort the bhikkhunis.
  5. Even if he has previously received the agreement, he should not exhort the bhikkhunis.
  6. He should not commit that same offense for which the Act of Censure was performed.
  7. He should not commit another offense of a similar kind.
  8. He should not commit any worse offense.
  9. He should not criticize the Act of Censure.
  10. He should not criticize those bhikkhus who performed the formal act.
  11. He should not suspend the Uposatha of a regular bhikkhu.
  12. He should not suspend the Pavarana of a regular bhikkhu.
  13. He should not issue a summons to a regular bhikkhu.
  14. He should not take precedence in the monastery.
  15. He should not ask for leave from a regular bhikkhu to charge him.
  16. He should not charge a regular bhikkhu.
  17. He should not remind a regular bhikkhu of an offense.
  18. He should not incite bhikkhus to quarrel.

Revocation of the Act of Censure

If the bhikkhus subjected to the Act of Censure conduct themselves rightly by fulfilling the aforementioned eighteen duties and request the Community to revoke the act, the Community should revoke the Act of Censure. Those bhikkhus should approach the Community, arrange their upper robes over one shoulder, pay homage at the feet of the senior bhikkhus, sit in the squatting position, and with joined palms, request the revocation of the Act of Censure by reciting the following passage three times:

Mayaṃ bhante saṅghena tajjanīyakammakatā. Sammā vattāma. Lomaṃ pātema. Netthāraṃ vattāma. Tajjanīyakammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācāma. Dutiyampi -pe- Tatiyampi -pe-

The formal text for Revocation:

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho. Ime (Paṇḍuka Lohitakā) bhikkhū saṅghena tajjanīyakammakkatā. Sammā vattanti. Lomaṃ pātenthi. Netthāraṃ vattanti. Tajjanīyakammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācanti. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīyakammaṃ paṭippassambheyya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho. Ime (Paṇḍuka Lohitakā) bhikkhū saṅghena tajjanīyakammakkatā. Sammā vattanti. Lomaṃ pātenthi. Netthāraṃ vattanti. Tajjanīyakammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācanti. Saṅgho (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīya kammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati. (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīya kammassa paṭippassaddhi, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya.

Dutiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho. Ime (Paṇḍuka Lohitakā) -pe- saṅgho (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīyakammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīya kammassa paṭippassambheti, yassāyasmato khamati (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīyakammassa paṭippassaddhi. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya.

Tatiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho. Ime (Paṇḍuka Lohitakā) bhikkhū -pe- tajjanīyakammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācanti. Saṅgho (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīyakammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīyakammassa paṭippassaddhi, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya.

Paṭippassaddhaṃ saṅghena (Paṇḍuka Lohitakānaṃ) bhikkhūnaṃ tajjanīyakammaṃ khamati saṅghassa. Tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

23.html.title: The Act of Guidance
23.html.body:
The Blessed One enjoined the Act of Guidance on account of the Bhikkhu Seyyasaka, who associated with laypeople in an unsuitable manner, who was foolish and inexperienced, and who for that very reason was frequently and repeatedly guilty of offenses, constantly troubling the Community with the granting of probation, sending back to the beginning, the granting of penance, and rehabilitation. The intent of the Act of Guidance is to mandate that a bhikkhu take dependence and live under a teacher. When performing it, bring the ignorant bhikkhu who is frequent in offenses into the midst of the Community, charge him, remind him of the offense, and having made him acknowledge the offense, the act should be carried out.

The formal text of the act is as follows:

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (sey‍yasako) bhikkhu bālo av‍yatto āpatti bahulo anapadāno gihī saṃsaṭṭho viharati ananulomikehi gihī saṃsaggehi. Apissu bhikkhu pakatā parivāsaṃ dentā mūlāya paṭikassantā mānattaṃ dentā abbhentā. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammaṃ karey‍ya nissāya te vat‍thabbanti. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Ayaṃ (sey‍yasako) bhikkhu bālo av‍yatto āpatti bahulo anapadāno gihī saṃsaṭṭho viharati ananulomikehi gihī saṃsaggehi. Apissu bhikkhu pakatā parivāsaṃ dentā mūlāya paṭikassantā mānattaṃ dentā abbhentā. Saṅgho (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammaṃ karoti nissāya te vat‍thabbanti. Yassāyasmato khamati (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kamma karaṇaṃ. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati so bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (sey‍yasako) bhikkhu bālo av‍yatto āpattibahulo anapadāno gihīsaṃsaṭṭho viharati ananulomikehi gihīsaṃsaggehi. Apissu bhikkhu pakatā parivāsaṃ dentā mūlāya paṭikassantā mānattaṃ dentā abbhentā. Saṅgho (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammaṃ karoti nissāya te vat‍thabbanti. Yassāyasmato khamati (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammassa karaṇaṃ nissāya te vat‍thabbanti, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante saṅgho. Ayaṃ (sey‍yasako) bhikkhu bālo av‍yatto āpatti bahulo anapadāno gihī saṃsaṭṭho viharati. Ananulomikehi gihī saṃsaggehi. Apissu bhikkhu pakatā parivāsaṃ dentā mūlāya paṭikassantā mānattaṃ dentā abbhentā. Saṅgho (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammaṃ karoti nissāya te vat‍thabbanti. Yassāyasmato khamati (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammassa karaṇaṃ. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Kataṃ saṅgheṇa (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammaṃ nissāya te vat‍thabbanti. Khamati saṅghassa. Tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

Revocation of the Act of Guidance.

A bhikkhu against whom the Act of Guidance has been performed must conduct himself in the same manner as a bhikkhu against whom the Act of Censure has been performed. While living in dependence on a teacher and fulfilling the eighteen duties, he must practice restraint and entreat the Community to revoke the Act of Guidance. Thereupon, the Community revokes the Act of Guidance.

The formula for requesting revocation:

Ahaṃ bhante saṅghena niyassa kamma kato sammā vattāmi. Lomaṃ pātemi, net‍thāraṃ vattāmi, niyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācāmi. Dutiyampi -pe- tatiyampi -pe-

The formal text for the revocation of the Act of Guidance.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (sey‍yasako) bhikkhu saṅghena niyassa kamma kato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Niyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (sey‍yakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammaṃ paṭippassambhey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (sey‍yasako) bhikkhu saṅghena niyassa kamma kato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Niyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhi. So tuṇhassa, yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante saṅgho, ayaṃ (sey‍yasako) bhikkhu saṅghena niyassa kammakato. Sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Niyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhi, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (sey‍yasako) bhikkhu saṅghena niyassa kammakato. Sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Niyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhi. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati. So bhāsey‍ya.

Paṭippassaddhaṃ saṅghena (sey‍yasakassa) bhikkhuno niyassa kammaṃ. Khamati saṅghassa tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

24.html.title: Act of Reconciliation (Patisāraṇīya Kamma)
24.html.body:
The Tathāgata prescribed the Act of Reconciliation on account of the Bhikkhu Sudhamma, who had reviled the householder Citta—a faithful devotee who served the Sangha—and subsequently abandoned his residence. This formal act is to be carried out against bhikkhus who insult, offend, or cause dissatisfaction among lay devotees who are faithful, settled in the Dispensation, and devoted to the Sasana. Bring the offending bhikkhu into the midst of the Community, charge him, make him remember the offense, and having made him acknowledge the transgression, perform the formal act.

The Formal Statement (Kammavācā)

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ayaṃ (Sudhammo) bhikkhu (Cittaṃ gahapatiṃ) saddhaṃ pasannaṃ dāyakaṃ kārakaṃ saṅghupaṭṭhākaṃ hīnena khuṃsesi. Hīnena vambhesi. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ. Saṅgho (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīya kammaṃ kareyya, (Citto te gahapati) khamāpetabboti. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho ayaṃ (Sudhammo) bhikkhu (Cittaṃ gahapatiṃ) saddhaṃ pasannaṃ dāyakaṃ kārakaṃ saṅghupaṭṭhānaṃ hīnena khuṃsesi. Hīnena vambhesi. Saṅgho (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīya kammaṃ karoti (Citto te gahapati) khamāpetabboti. Yassāyasmato khamati (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīyassa kammassakaraṇaṃ. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya.

Dutiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu -pe-

Tatiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ayaṃ (Sudhammo) bhikkhu (Cittaṃ gahapatiṃ) saddhaṃ pasannaṃ dāyakaṃ kārakaṃ saṅghupaṭṭhākaṃ hīnena khuṃsesi. Hīnena vambhesi. Saṅgho (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīya kammaṃ karoti (Citto te gahapati) khamāpetabboti. Yassāyasmato khamati (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīya kammassa karaṇaṃ (Citto te gahapati) khamāpetabboti. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya.

kataṃ saṅghena (sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīya kammaṃ (citto te gahapati) khamāpetabboti. Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

A bhikkhu against whom the Act of Reconciliation has been performed must observe the eighteen duties (vatta) prescribed for those undergoing the Act of Censure (Tajjaniya Kamma) until the act is revoked by the Community. The Community revokes the act only after the bhikkhu has asked for forgiveness from the person he offended and has restored their confidence. After the Act of Reconciliation was performed against Bhikkhu Sudhamma for offending the householder Citta, he went to Macchikāsaṇḍa to ask for forgiveness. However, being overcome with remorse, he was unable to speak to the householder Citta and returned to Sāvatthī.

When the bhikkhus inquired whether the householder Citta had forgiven him, Bhikkhu Sudhamma stated he was unable to ask. When this was reported to the Tathāgata, He allowed the Sangha to appoint a companion messenger (anudūta) to assist the bhikkhu in asking for forgiveness. In granting a companion messenger, one must first request a bhikkhu to perform the duty, obtain his consent, and then formally appoint him.

Formal Act for Granting a Companion Messenger

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (Tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno anudūtaṃ dadeyya (Cittaṃ gahapatiṃ) khamāpetuṃ, esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho. Saṅgho (Tissaṃ) bhikkhuṃ (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno anudūtaṃ deti (Cittaṃ gahapatiṃ) khamāpetuṃ. Yassāyasmato khamati (Tissassa) bhikkhuno (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno anudūtassa dānaṃ (Cittaṃ gahapatiṃ) khamāpetuṃ. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya.

Dinno saṅghena (Tisso) bhikkhu (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno anudūto (Cittaṃ gahapatiṃ) khamāpetuṃ. Khamati saṅghassa. Tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

The bhikkhu against whom the Act of Reconciliation was performed must go to the offended householder with the companion messenger appointed by the Community and beg for forgiveness. If the layperson does not forgive and regain confidence, the companion messenger should say: "Householder, please forgive this bhikkhu; this bhikkhu desires your goodwill."

If the householder still does not forgive, he should say: "Householder, I ask you to forgive this bhikkhu." If he still does not forgive, he should say: "Householder, forgive this bhikkhu at the request of the Community." If he still does not forgive, then in the sight and hearing of the householder, the offending bhikkhu should be made to arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, sit in the squatting position, join his hands in reverence, and confess his offense.

Revocation of the Act of Reconciliation

When the bhikkhu against whom the Act of Reconciliation was performed has obtained forgiveness from the lay devotee he insulted, has restored the devotee's confidence, and has satisfied the minds of the bhikkhus, he should approach the Community. He must arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay homage at the feet of the senior bhikkhus, sit in the squatting position, join his hands in reverence, and request the revocation of the Act of Reconciliation using the following words:

Ahaṃ bhante! Saṅghena paṭisāraṇīya kammakato. Sammā vattāmi. Lomaṃ pātemi. Netthāraṃ vattāmi. Paṭisāraṇīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācāmi. Dutiyampi -pe- Tatiyampi -pe-

Formal Act for the Revocation of the Act of Reconciliation

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ayaṃ (Sudhammo) bhikkhu saṅghena paṭisāraṇīya kammakato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Netthāraṃ vattati. Paṭisāraṇīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīya kammaṃ paṭippassambheyya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho, ayaṃ (Sudhammo) bhikkhu saṅghena paṭisāraṇīya kammakato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Netthāraṃ vattati. Paṭisāraṇīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīya kammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhi. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati. So bhāseyya.

Dutiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi. -pe-

Tatiyampi etamatthaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! Saṅgho. Ayaṃ (Sudhammo) bhikkhu saṅghena paṭisāraṇīya kammakato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Netthāraṃ vattati. Paṭisāraṇīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīya kammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati. (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhi. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāseyya.

Paṭippassaddhaṃ saṅghena (Sudhammassa) bhikkhuno paṭisāraṇīya kammaṃ. Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.
24.html.nextLabel: Act of Turning Down the Bowl

25.html.title: The Act of Turning Over the Alms-bowl (Pattanikkujjana Kamma)
25.html.body:
The Act of Turning Over the Alms-bowl is a formal act performed as a penalty for laypeople who have wronged bhikkhus. Its purpose is the boycotting of the offending layperson by the Community. A layperson for whom the bowl has been turned over must be avoided by the bhikkhus. No bhikkhu should accept anything offered by him. Even when wandering for alms, his house should be passed by. This act was instituted on account of the Licchavi named Vaddha, who brought a false accusation of a breach of morality against the Elder Dabba Mallaputta.

Aṭṭhahi bhikkhave! aṅgehi samannāgatassa upāsakassa patto nikukjjitabbo. Bhikkhūnaṃ alābhāya parisakkati. Bhikkhūnaṃ anat‍thāya parisakkati. Bhikkhūnaṃ avāsāya parisakkati. Bhikkhūnaṃ akkosati paribhāsati bhikkhu bhikkhūhi bhedeti. Buddhassa avaṇṇaṃ bhāsati. Dhammassa avaṇṇaṃ bhāsati. Saṅghassa avaṇṇaṃ bhāsati. Anujānāmi bhikkhave! imehi aṭṭhahaṅgehi samannāgatassa upāsakassa pattaṃ nikkujjituṃ.

As declared by the Blessed One, the Act of Turning Over the Alms-bowl should be performed against a lay follower who possesses these eight qualities: he strives for the loss of gains for bhikkhus; he strives for the harm of bhikkhus; he strives for the lack of residence for bhikkhus; he reviles and insults bhikkhus; he causes dissension among bhikkhus; he speaks in dispraise of the Buddha; he speaks in dispraise of the Dhamma; he speaks in dispraise of the Sangha.

The Formal Act for Turning Over the Alms-bowl

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. (vaḍḍho licchavī) āyasmantaṃ (dabbaṃ mallaputtaṃ) amūlikāya sīlavipattiyā anuddhaṃseti. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ. Saṅgho (vaḍḍhassa licchavissa) pattaṃ nikkujjey‍ya asambhogaṃ saṅghena karey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. (vaḍḍho licchavī) āyasmantaṃ (dabbaṃ mallaputtaṃ) amūlikāya sīlavipattiyā anuddhaṃseti. Saṅgho (vaḍḍhassa licchavissa) pattaṃ nikkujjati, sambhogaṃ saṅghena karoti. Yassāyasmato khamati (vaḍḍhassa licchavissa) pattassa nikkujjanā asambhogaṃ saṅghena karaṇaṃ. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Nikkujjito saṅghena (vaḍḍhassa licchavissa) patto asambhogo saṅghena, khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

The Removal of the Turning Over of the Alms-bowl

If the person against whom the Act of Turning Over the Alms-bowl was performed desires its removal, have him acknowledge the fault and obtain forgiveness from the Community. Have him approach the Community, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay homage at the feet of the bhikkhus, sit on his haunches, and with hands joined in reverence, request the turning upright of the alms-bowl by reciting this formula:

Saṅghena me bhante! patto nikkujjito, asambhogomhi saṅghena, so’haṃ bhante sammā vattāmi. Lomaṃ pātemi. Netthāraṃ vattāmi, saṅghaṃ pattukkujjanaṃ yācāmi. Dutiyampi -pe- tatiyampi -pe-

The Formal Act for Turning the Alms-bowl Upright

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Saṅghena (vaḍḍhassa licchavissa) patto nikkujjito asambhogo saṅghena, so sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Saṅghaṃ pattakkujjanaṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ. Saṅgho (vaḍḍhassa licchavissa) pattaṃ ukkujjey‍ya. Sambhogaṃ saṅghena karey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Saṅghena (vaḍḍhassa licchavissa) patto nikkujjito asambhogo saṅghena, so sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Saṅghaṃ pattukkajjanaṃ yācati. Saṅgho (vaḍḍhassa licchavissa) pattaṃ ukkujjati, sambhogaṃ saṅghena karoti. Yassāyasmato khamati (vaḍḍhassa licchavissa) pattassa ukkujjanā sambhogaṃ saṅghena karaṇaṃ, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Ukkujjito saṅghena (vaḍḍhassa licchavissa) patto, sambhogo saṅghena, khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

26-1.html.title: The Act of Suspension for Not Acknowledging an Offense.
26-1.html.body:
While the Blessed One was staying at Ghositarama in Kosambi, the Elder Channa committed an offense, and when told by the bhikkhus that he had fallen into an offense, he did not wish to acknowledge it. When the bhikkhus reported this to the Tathagata, He prescribed the "Act of Suspension" for the Elder Channa. The purpose of the Act of Suspension is for the Community to exclude the offending bhikkhu from communion. When performing it, the bhikkhu who does not acknowledge the offense must be accused, reminded of the offense, and charged before the act is carried out.

The Procedural Text (Kamma-vākya)

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu āpattiṃ āpajjit‍vā na icchati āpattiṃ passituṃ, yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammaṃ karey‍ya asambhogaṃ saṅghena, esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu āpattiṃ āpajjit‍vā na icchati āpattiṃ passituṃ. Saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammaṃ karoti asambhogaṃ saṅghena. Yassāyasmato khamati (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyassa kammassa karaṇaṃ. Asambhogaṃ saṅghena, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe-

Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu āpattiṃ āpajjit‍vā na icchati āpattiṃ passituṃ. Saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammaṃ karoti asambhogaṃ saṅghena. Yassāyasmato khamati (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyassa kammassa karaṇaṃ. Asambhogaṃ saṅghena so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati. So bhāsey‍ya.

Kataṃ saṅghena (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammaṃ asambhogaṃ saṅghena khamati saṅghassa. Tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

Duties to be observed by a suspended bhikkhu.

  1. He should not confer the Full Ordination as a preceptor or teacher.
  2. He should not give dependence (nissaya).
  3. He should not take a novice as a preceptor.
  4. He should not accept authorization to exhort bhikkhunis.
  5. Even if authorized, he should not exhort bhikkhunis.
  6. He should not commit that same offense for which the Act of Suspension was performed.
  7. He should not commit any other similar offense.
  8. He should not commit any worse offense.
  9. He should not disparage the formal act.
  10. He should not disparage the bhikkhus who performed the act.

11-21. He should not accept from a regular bhikkhu: salutation, rising from the seat, salutation with joined palms, proper services such as fanning, the preparation of seats, the preparation of beds, the setting out of water for washing the feet, the setting out of footstools, the setting out of foot-mats, the receiving of bowl and robes, or the scrubbing of the back during a bath.

  1. He should not accuse a regular bhikkhu of a failure in virtue.
  2. He should not accuse him of a failure in conduct.
  3. He should not accuse him of a failure in view.
  4. He should not accuse him of a failure in livelihood.
  5. He should not cause a regular bhikkhu to break with other bhikkhus.
  6. He should not wear white clothes or other lay garments.
  7. He should not wear the robes of sectarians, such as those made of kusa grass.
  8. He should associate with bhikkhus.
  9. He should train in the training of bhikkhus.
  10. He should not live in a residence under the same roof as a regular bhikkhu.
  11. He should not live in a non-residence under the same roof as a regular bhikkhu.
  12. He should not live in either a residence or a non-residence under the same roof as a regular bhikkhu.
  13. On seeing a regular bhikkhu, he should rise from his seat.
  14. He should not show disrespect to a regular bhikkhu, whether inside or outside the monastery.
  15. He should not suspend the Uposatha of a regular bhikkhu.
  16. He should not suspend the Pavarana of a regular bhikkhu.
  17. He should not issue a formal summons (savacanīya) to a regular bhikkhu.
  18. He should not take a position of leadership in the monastery.
  19. He should not ask for leave to accuse.
  20. He should not bring an accusation.
  21. He should not remind another of an offense.
  22. He should not incite bhikkhus to quarrel with other bhikkhus.

Revocation of the Act of Suspension for Not Acknowledging an Offense.

The suspended bhikkhu, having fulfilled his duties and satisfied the Community, should approach the Community, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay homage at the feet of the senior bhikkhus, sit in the squatting position, and request the revocation of the act by reciting the following passage:

Ahaṃ bhante! saṅghena āpattiyā adassena ukkhepanīya kammakato sammā vattāmi. Lomaṃ pātemi. Net‍thāraṃ vattāmi. Āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācāmi. Dutiyampi -pe- tatiyampi -pe-

The Procedural Text (Kamma-vākya)

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu saṅghena āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammakato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammaṃ paṭippassambhey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho. Ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu saṅghena āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammakato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīya kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasamato khamati (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīya kammassa paṭippassaddhi, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu -pe-

Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammakato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyassakammassa paṭippassaddhi. So tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Paṭippassaddhaṃ saṅghena (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā adassane ukkhepanīyakammaṃ. Khamati saṅghassa. Tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.
26-1.html.nextLabel: The Act of Suspension for Not Making Amends for an Offense.

26-2.html.title: The Act of Suspension for a Bhikkhu who does not wish to make amends for an offense.
26-2.html.body:
While the Blessed One was staying at Ghositarama in Kosambi, the Elder Channa fell into an offense and did not wish to make amends for it. On that account, the Tathagata prescribed that the Community perform the Act of Suspension for not making amends for an offense against the Elder Channa, resulting in non-communion with the Community. This act must be performed by summoning the bhikkhu into the midst of the Community, accusing him, reminding him of the fault, and pointing out the offense.

The Formal Act (Kamma-vācā)

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu āpattiṃ āpajjit‍vā na icchati āpattiṃ paṭikātuṃ. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīya kammaṃ karey‍ya asambhogaṃ saṅghena. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu āpattiṃ āpajjit‍vā na icchati āpattiṃ paṭikātuṃ. Saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīya kammaṃ karoti. Asambhogaṃ saṅghena. Yassāyasmato khamati. (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyassa kammassa karaṇaṃ asambhogaṃ saṅghena, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu -pe-

Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu āpattiṃ āpajjit‍vā na icchati āpattiṃ paṭikātuṃ. Saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyakammaṃ karoti. Asambhogaṃ saṅghena yassāyasmato khamati (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyassa kammassa karaṇaṃ asambhogaṃ saṅghena, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati, so bhāsey‍ya.

Kataṃ saṅghena (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyakammaṃ asambhogaṃ saṅghena. Khamati saṅghassa. Tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṃ dhārayāmi.

Revocation of the Act of Suspension.

When the Act of Suspension for not making amends for an offense has been performed, the bhikkhu must conduct himself according to the forty-three duties, satisfy the Community, approach the Community, pay homage at the feet of the senior bhikkhus, sit in the squatting position, and with hands joined in reverence, request the revocation of the act using the following formula. When this is done, the Community shall revoke the act.

Ahaṃ bhante! saṅghena āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyakammakato sammā vattāmi. Lomaṃ pātemi. Netthāraṃ vattāmi. Āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācāmi. Dutiyampi -pe- tatiyampi -pe-

The Formal Act for Revocation.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu saṅghena āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyakammakato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīya kammaṃ paṭippassambhey‍ya. Esā ñatti.

Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, ayaṃ (channo) bhikkhu saṅghena āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyakammakato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyakammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhi, so tuṇhassa. Yassa nakkhamati. So bhāsey‍ya.

Dutiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me -pe-

Tatiyampi etamat‍thaṃ vadāmi. Suṇātu me bhante! saṅgho, aya (channo) bhikkhu saṅghena āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyakammakato sammā vattati. Lomaṃ pāteti. Net‍thāraṃ vattati. Āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhiṃ yācati. Saṅgho (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyakammaṃ paṭippassambheti. Yassāyasmato khamati (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyassa kammassa paṭippassaddhi. So tuṇhassa, yassa nakkhamati. So bhāsey‍ya.

Paṭippassaddhaṃ saṅghena (channassa) bhikkhuno āpattiyā appaṭikamme ukkhepanīyakammaṃ. Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī. Evametaṃ dhārayāmi.
